           <div itemprop="articleBody">
             
  <section id="load-schema">
<h1>Load schema<a class="headerlink" href="#load-schema" title="Link to this heading"></a></h1>
<p>The Load Schema block is used to load a schema from the “metadata” database and
transform it to a JSON schema which can be used to define a dynamic form.</p>
<section id="typical-usage">
<h2>Typical usage<a class="headerlink" href="#typical-usage" title="Link to this heading"></a></h2>
<p>Used as a sub-block of the “Form” schemaGetter definition.</p>
<div class="highlight-javascript notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span>
</pre></div>
</div>
<dl>
<dt>{</dt><dd><p>“type”: “form”,
“label”: “Save”,
“hasSubmit”: false,
“schemaGetter”: {</p>
<blockquote>
<div><dl>
<dt>“blocks”: [</dt><dd><dl class="simple">
<dt>{</dt><dd><p>“type”: “load-schema”,
“adapterName”: “schemas”,
“schemaGetter”: “context.schema.name”</p>
</dd>
</dl>
<p>}</p>
</dd>
</dl>
<p>]</p>
</div></blockquote>
<p>}</p>
</dd>
</dl>
<p>}</p>
</section>
<section id="default-config">
<h2>Default config<a class="headerlink" href="#default-config" title="Link to this heading"></a></h2>
<div class="highlight-javascript notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><span class="p">{</span>
<span class="w">  </span><span class="s2">&quot;type&quot;</span><span class="o">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;load-schema&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">  </span><span class="s2">&quot;schema&quot;</span><span class="o">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s1">&#39;schema&#39;</span>
<span class="p">}</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
</section>
<section id="supported-properties">
<h2>Supported properties<a class="headerlink" href="#supported-properties" title="Link to this heading"></a></h2>
<blockquote>
<div><dl class="simple">
<dt><strong>adapterName</strong>  - the name of the adapter to use.</dt><dd><p><strong>schema</strong> - the name of the schema to use
<strong>schemaGetter</strong> - a JMESPath expression to get the schema from the data</p>
</dd>
</dl>
<p>Use either schema or schemaGetter. schemaGetter will override schema.</p>
</div></blockquote>
</section>
<section id="schema-data-structure-json-schema">
<h2>Schema data structure (JSON schema)<a class="headerlink" href="#schema-data-structure-json-schema" title="Link to this heading"></a></h2>
<p>This block expects data in a structure like the example below:</p>
<div class="highlight-json notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><span class="p">{</span>
<span class="w">      </span><span class="nt">&quot;type&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;object&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">      </span><span class="nt">&quot;properties&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="p">{</span>
<span class="w">          </span><span class="nt">&quot;name&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="p">{</span>
<span class="w">              </span><span class="nt">&quot;type&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;string&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">              </span><span class="nt">&quot;title&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;Schema name&quot;</span>
<span class="w">          </span><span class="p">},</span>
<span class="w">          </span><span class="nt">&quot;description&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="p">{</span>
<span class="w">              </span><span class="nt">&quot;type&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;string&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">              </span><span class="nt">&quot;title&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;Description&quot;</span>
<span class="w">          </span><span class="p">},</span>
<span class="w">          </span><span class="nt">&quot;label&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="p">{</span>
<span class="w">              </span><span class="nt">&quot;type&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;string&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">              </span><span class="nt">&quot;title&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;Label property&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">              </span><span class="nt">&quot;description&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;key of propery to use as item label&quot;</span>
<span class="w">          </span><span class="p">},</span>
<span class="w">          </span><span class="nt">&quot;properties&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="p">{</span>
<span class="w">              </span><span class="nt">&quot;type&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;array&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">              </span><span class="nt">&quot;title&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;Properties&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">              </span><span class="nt">&quot;items&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="p">{</span>
<span class="w">                  </span><span class="nt">&quot;type&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;object&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">                  </span><span class="nt">&quot;properties&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="p">{</span>
<span class="w">                      </span><span class="nt">&quot;key&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="p">{</span>
<span class="w">                          </span><span class="nt">&quot;type&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;string&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">                          </span><span class="nt">&quot;title&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;Key&quot;</span>
<span class="w">                      </span><span class="p">},</span>
<span class="w">                      </span><span class="nt">&quot;title&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="p">{</span>
<span class="w">                          </span><span class="nt">&quot;type&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;string&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">                          </span><span class="nt">&quot;title&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;Title&quot;</span>
<span class="w">                      </span><span class="p">},</span>
<span class="w">                      </span><span class="nt">&quot;type&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="p">{</span>
<span class="w">                          </span><span class="nt">&quot;type&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;string&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">                          </span><span class="nt">&quot;title&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;Type&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">                          </span><span class="nt">&quot;enum&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="p">[</span>
<span class="w">                              </span><span class="s2">&quot;Text&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">                              </span><span class="s2">&quot;Number&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">                              </span><span class="s2">&quot;Date&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">                              </span><span class="s2">&quot;Reference&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">                              </span><span class="s2">&quot;Object&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">                              </span><span class="s2">&quot;List&quot;</span>
<span class="w">                          </span><span class="p">]</span>
<span class="w">                      </span><span class="p">},</span>
<span class="w">                      </span><span class="nt">&quot;config&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="p">{</span>
<span class="w">                          </span><span class="nt">&quot;type&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;string&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">                          </span><span class="nt">&quot;title&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;Config&quot;</span>
<span class="w">                      </span><span class="p">}</span>
<span class="w">                  </span><span class="p">}</span>
<span class="w">              </span><span class="p">}</span>
<span class="w">          </span><span class="p">}</span>
<span class="w">      </span><span class="p">}</span>
<span class="w">  </span><span class="p">}</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
</section>
</section>

  </form>
</div>
        </div><div class="wy-menu wy-menu-vertical" data-spy="affix" role="navigation" aria-label="Navigation menu">

          <div role="main" class="document" itemscope="itemscope" itemtype="http://schema.org/Article">
           <div itemprop="articleBody">
             
  <section id="map">
<h1>Map<a class="headerlink" href="#map" title="Link to this heading"></a></h1>
<p>The map block allows embedding a map for display of geocoded data.
Leaflet.JS is used to display the map, and some Leaflet.JS customisation
options are supported.</p>
<p>The <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">height</span></code> in pixels of the block (default height 500px),
and the initial <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">zoom</span></code> level (default zoom = 8)
are provided as configuration options to the block. You can also optionally
provide a <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">latlng</span></code> config option for the default map center point.
The map defaults to centering at [51.505, -0.09].</p>
<section id="examples">
<h2>Examples<a class="headerlink" href="#examples" title="Link to this heading"></a></h2>
<p>The map block expects incoming data to be an array of objects that
have <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">lat</span></code>, <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">long</span></code>, and <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">label</span></code> properties. Here is an example
of a mapping that produces appropriate data:</p>
<div class="highlight-json notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><span class="p">{</span>
<span class="w">    </span><span class="nt">&quot;type&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;mapping&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">    </span><span class="nt">&quot;mapping&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;data[?recovered &gt; `20`].{ lat: lat, long: long, label: join(&#39; &#39;, [to_string(recovered), &#39;recovered in&#39;, combinedKey][? @ != null]) }&quot;</span>
<span class="p">}</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
<p>Example map block configuration:</p>
<div class="highlight-json notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><span class="p">{</span>
<span class="w">    </span><span class="nt">&quot;type&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;map&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">    </span><span class="nt">&quot;height&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="mi">500</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">    </span><span class="nt">&quot;zoom&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="mi">2</span>
<span class="p">}</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
</section>
</section>

          <div role="main" class="document" itemscope="itemscope" itemtype="http://schema.org/Article">
           <div itemprop="articleBody">
             
  <section id="convert-template-records-into-form-schemas">
<h1>Convert template records into form schemas<a class="headerlink" href="#convert-template-records-into-form-schemas" title="Link to this heading"></a></h1>
<p>Makes form schemas from existing JSON data “templates”.
Rather than records being fixed in stone with no way to add new properties,
we enable making forms dynamically that build upon existing JSON data.</p>
<p>Using existing JSON records as a template, we can create new forms for making records of data.
How? By using transformations that users can configure to, adding new fields, and rename keys.
To make descendants of templates uniquely identifiable, we can also generate new UUIDs.
By transforming existing JSON record objects using user defined templates, data is changed into a form templates using configurable rules.</p>
<section id="background-context">
<h2>Background context:<a class="headerlink" href="#background-context" title="Link to this heading"></a></h2>
<p>Users can make schemas using the schema builder, and make records for the schemas.
The records can themselves be templates for other forms.
Users can make records with the Form block and save them as records for use as templates.
Example use-case: a Content Management System (CMS) can have templates for different blocks of content, text, images, videos etc.
Using this, the CMS can then use the templates to create new content.
The load-schema block can allow adding new JSON properties to these records.
We convert template objects so further information can be added to the form.
We make new JSON schemas for the Form block to use to fill extra details in,</p>
</section>
<section id="terminology">
<h2>Terminology:<a class="headerlink" href="#terminology" title="Link to this heading"></a></h2>
<p>We use the term “template” to refer to a JSON object that is used as a basis for making a form.
The term “form” is used to refer to a JSON object that is used to make a record using the form block.</p>
</section>
<section id="currently-focused-on-the-cms-use-case">
<h2>Currently focused on the CMS use-case:<a class="headerlink" href="#currently-focused-on-the-cms-use-case" title="Link to this heading"></a></h2>
<p>Currently, the implementation is focused on the CMS (Content Management System) use-case.</p>
<p>Given JSON record, created from an existing form like this:</p>
<div class="highlight-json notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><span class="p">{</span>
<span class="w">    </span><span class="nt">&quot;websitemodel&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="p">{</span>
<span class="w">        </span><span class="nt">&quot;blockmodel&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="p">[</span>
<span class="w">            </span><span class="p">{</span>
<span class="w">                </span><span class="nt">&quot;uuid&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;c5bfac02-f0b6-4c31-8fdf-18de02667ee9&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">                </span><span class="nt">&quot;src&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;https://placekitten.com/200/300&quot;</span>
<span class="w">            </span><span class="p">}</span>
<span class="w">        </span><span class="p">]</span>
<span class="w">    </span><span class="p">}</span>
<span class="p">}</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
<p>The load-schema block can be loaded and configured like this:</p>
<div class="highlight-json notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><span class="p">{</span>
<span class="w">  </span><span class="nt">&quot;block&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;load-schema&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">  </span><span class="nt">&quot;templateToSchema&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="p">{</span>
<span class="w">    </span><span class="nt">&quot;blockTypeDefaults&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="p">{</span>
<span class="w">      </span><span class="nt">&quot;c5bfac02-f0b6-4c31-8fdf-18de02667ee9&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="p">{</span>
<span class="w">        </span><span class="nt">&quot;type&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;object&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">        </span><span class="nt">&quot;properties&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="p">{</span>
<span class="w">          </span><span class="nt">&quot;src&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="p">{</span>
<span class="w">            </span><span class="nt">&quot;type&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;string&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">            </span><span class="nt">&quot;title&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;Image URL&quot;</span>
<span class="w">          </span><span class="p">}</span>
<span class="w">        </span><span class="p">}</span>
<span class="w">      </span><span class="p">}</span>
<span class="w">    </span><span class="p">},</span>
<span class="w">    </span><span class="nt">&quot;convertables&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="p">{</span>
<span class="w">      </span><span class="nt">&quot;websitemodel&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;website-content&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">      </span><span class="nt">&quot;blockmodel&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;block-content&quot;</span>
<span class="w">    </span><span class="p">}</span>
<span class="w">  </span><span class="p">}</span>
<span class="p">}</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
<p>It will use that to produce a new JSON schema for the form block to use, something like this:</p>
<div class="highlight-json notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><span class="p">{</span>
<span class="w">    </span><span class="nt">&quot;type&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;object&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">    </span><span class="nt">&quot;properties&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="p">{</span>
<span class="w">        </span><span class="nt">&quot;website-content&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="p">{</span>
<span class="w">            </span><span class="nt">&quot;type&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;object&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">            </span><span class="nt">&quot;properties&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="p">{</span>
<span class="w">                </span><span class="nt">&quot;block-content&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="p">{</span>
<span class="w">                    </span><span class="nt">&quot;type&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;object&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">                    </span><span class="nt">&quot;properties&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="p">{</span>
<span class="w">                        </span><span class="nt">&quot;b74e30a7-a052-4331-86eb-ba2eb7a31ce4&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="p">{</span>
<span class="w">                            </span><span class="nt">&quot;type&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;object&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">                            </span><span class="nt">&quot;properties&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="p">{</span>
<span class="w">                                </span><span class="nt">&quot;src&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="p">{</span>
<span class="w">                                    </span><span class="nt">&quot;type&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;string&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">                                    </span><span class="nt">&quot;title&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;Image URL&quot;</span>
<span class="w">                                </span><span class="p">}</span>
<span class="w">                            </span><span class="p">}</span>
<span class="w">                        </span><span class="p">}</span>
<span class="w">                    </span><span class="p">}</span>
<span class="w">                </span><span class="p">}</span>
<span class="w">            </span><span class="p">}</span>
<span class="w">        </span><span class="p">}</span>
<span class="w">    </span><span class="p">}</span>
<span class="p">}</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
<p>It uses the existing loaded JSON record data as context, and output JSON Schema as config for use by the Form block (to allow editing).</p>
<p>It can also be saved as a JSON schema, to later load and make multiple “child” documents.</p>
<p>Note the UUIDs in the JSON Schema are generated automatically for the “block-content” property items.</p>
</section>
</section>


           </div>
          </div>

  <ul class="wy-breadcrumbs">
      <li><a href="../../index.html" class="icon icon-home" aria-label="Home"></a></li>
      <li class="breadcrumb-item active">Link Action block</li>
      <li class="wy-breadcrumbs-aside">

</div>
          <div role="main" class="document" itemscope="itemscope" itemtype="http://schema.org/Article">
           <div itemprop="articleBody">
             
  <section id="link-action-block">
<h1>Link Action block<a class="headerlink" href="#link-action-block" title="Link to this heading"></a></h1>
<p>The Link Action block provides a user-friendly button that links to other Flows.</p>
<p>The block is implemented as a link and not a real button, and benefits from the usual behaviour of browser links, for example, the user can choose how Flows are opened, such as the same in the same tab, or a new tab, based on their browser configuration, and users may notice the destination Flow is shown when hovering over the link, this allows the user to directly see where the button would navigate to (which is an improvement over the Launch block, see below).</p>
<section id="default-configuration">
<h2>Default configuration<a class="headerlink" href="#default-configuration" title="Link to this heading"></a></h2>
<div class="highlight-json notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><span class="p">{</span>
<span class="w">  </span><span class="nt">&quot;type&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;link-action&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">  </span><span class="nt">&quot;label&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;Open Flow&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">  </span><span class="nt">&quot;adapterName&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;adapterName&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">  </span><span class="nt">&quot;workflowId&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;workflowId&quot;</span>
<span class="p">}</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
</section>
<section id="supported-properties">
<h2>Supported Properties<a class="headerlink" href="#supported-properties" title="Link to this heading"></a></h2>
<ul class="simple">
<li><p><strong>label</strong> (string): The text displayed on the button-like link. Defaults to “Link”.</p></li>
<li><p><strong>adapterName</strong> (string): The adapter name of the target workflow. This property is ignored if a value is specified by <cite>adapterNameGetter</cite> instead.</p></li>
<li><p><strong>adapterNameGetter</strong> (string): A JMESPath expression that resolves to the adapter name of the target workflow. This allows for dynamic resolution of the adapter name from the data and context. If not set, will use the <cite>adapterName</cite> property instead.</p></li>
<li><p><strong>workflowId</strong> (string): The workflow ID of the target workflow. This property is ignored if a value is specified by <cite>workflowIdGetter</cite> instead.</p></li>
<li><p><strong>workflowIdGetter</strong> (string): A JMESPath expression that resolves to the workflow ID of the target workflow. This allows for dynamic resolution of the workflow ID from the data and context. If not set, will use the <cite>workflowId</cite> property instead.</p></li>
</ul>
</section>
<section id="usage">
<h2>Usage<a class="headerlink" href="#usage" title="Link to this heading"></a></h2>
<p>The block can be configured for use in a <em>static</em> or <em>dynamic</em> way.</p>
<p><strong>Static Flow links:</strong></p>
<p>Uses hardcoded values for <cite>adapterName</cite> and <cite>workflowId</cite> to create a static link to a specific Flow. For example:</p>
<div class="highlight-json notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><span class="p">{</span>
<span class="w">    </span><span class="nt">&quot;type&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;link-action&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">    </span><span class="nt">&quot;label&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;View Details&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">    </span><span class="nt">&quot;adapterName&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;myAdapter&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">    </span><span class="nt">&quot;workflowId&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;myWorkflow&quot;</span>
<span class="p">}</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
<p>In this example, it will create a link with the label “View Details” that points to the <cite>/myAdapter/myWorkflow</cite> route.</p>
<p><strong>Dynamic Flow links within a grid:</strong></p>
<p>This example demonstrates using the <cite>LinkActionComponent</cite> within a grid cell to link to individual Flows.</p>
<ul class="simple">
<li><p>Use JMESPath expressions in <cite>adapterNameGetter</cite> and <cite>workflowIdGetter</cite> to dynamically generate the link URL based on the data provided to the block (in this case the data from the Grid).</p></li>
<li><p>Embed the block within the <cite>cellRendererParams</cite> of a grid column definition to provide a clickable link for each row.</p></li>
</ul>
<div class="highlight-json notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><span class="p">{</span>
<span class="w">    </span><span class="nt">&quot;headerName&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;Operations&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">    </span><span class="nt">&quot;cellRenderer&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;workflowRenderer&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">    </span><span class="nt">&quot;cellRendererParams&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="p">{</span>
<span class="w">        </span><span class="nt">&quot;blocks&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="p">[</span>
<span class="w">            </span><span class="p">{</span>
<span class="w">                </span><span class="nt">&quot;type&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;link-action&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">                </span><span class="nt">&quot;label&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;Launch&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">                </span><span class="nt">&quot;adapterNameGetter&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;data.adapterName&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">                </span><span class="nt">&quot;workflowIdGetter&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;data.id&quot;</span>
<span class="w">            </span><span class="p">}</span>
<span class="w">        </span><span class="p">]</span>
<span class="w">    </span><span class="p">}</span>
<span class="p">}</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
<p>In this configuration:
- <cite>data.adapterName</cite> and <cite>data.id</cite> are JMESPath expressions that retrieve the adapter name and workflow ID from the current row’s data.
- The generated link for each row will point to the corresponding Flow.</p>
<p><strong>Dynamic Flow links using context:</strong></p>
<div class="highlight-json notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><span class="p">{</span>
<span class="w">    </span><span class="nt">&quot;type&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;link-action&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">    </span><span class="nt">&quot;label&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;Login&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">    </span><span class="nt">&quot;adapterNameGetter&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;context.app.adapterName&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">    </span><span class="nt">&quot;workflowId&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;login&quot;</span>
<span class="p">}</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
<p>In this example, <cite>context.app.adapterName</cite> is a JMESPath expression that retrieves the adapter name from the Flow context, allowing for dynamic resolution of the adapter name, so this common pattern could be used in multiple Flows.  The <cite>workflowId</cite> is statically set to “login”.</p>
</section>
<section id="refactoring-note">
<h2>Refactoring note<a class="headerlink" href="#refactoring-note" title="Link to this heading"></a></h2>
<p>Existing Flows that use a combination of an <a class="reference internal" href="actions.html"><span class="doc">Actions block</span></a> and a <a class="reference internal" href="launch_block.html"><span class="doc">Launch Block</span></a> to open a Flow, can be replaced by the Link Action block to provide the user with more control, allowing the user to be aware of where the link will go to, and avoiding forcing navigation away from the current tab - the Link Action block allows users to open Flows in new tabs or windows, based on their browser configuration.</p>
</section>
</section>


           </div>
          </div>

          <div role="main" class="document" itemscope="itemscope" itemtype="http://schema.org/Article">
           <div itemprop="articleBody">
             
  <section id="comparison">
<h1>Comparison<a class="headerlink" href="#comparison" title="Link to this heading"></a></h1>
<p>Perform a simple operation, comparing a source value with a set of target values.</p>
<p>The comparison block works through each of a set of operations, and when
it finds a successful match, returns the specified output.</p>
<p>Once a match is found, processing stops. If no match is found, a default is returned.</p>
<p>The output of the block will replace “data”.</p>
<section id="configuration">
<h2>Configuration<a class="headerlink" href="#configuration" title="Link to this heading"></a></h2>
<p>The comparison block is configured by a custom form.</p>
<section id="general-value-types">
<h3>(General) Value Types<a class="headerlink" href="#general-value-types" title="Link to this heading"></a></h3>
<p>The comparison block assumes that target, output and default values are strings by default.
However, it is possible to specify that these are JMESPath values, which means that
they will be passed through the mapper to retrieve data, or perform additional operations.</p>
</section>
<section id="block-setup">
<h3>Block Setup<a class="headerlink" href="#block-setup" title="Link to this heading"></a></h3>
<section id="source">
<h4>Source<a class="headerlink" href="#source" title="Link to this heading"></a></h4>
<p>The source that we will be comparing against. This is a JMESpath expression.</p>
</section>
<section id="default">
<h4>Default<a class="headerlink" href="#default" title="Link to this heading"></a></h4>
<p>If no match is set, the default is returned.</p>
</section>
</section>
<section id="comparison-definitions">
<h3>Comparison definitions<a class="headerlink" href="#comparison-definitions" title="Link to this heading"></a></h3>
<section id="operator">
<h4>Operator<a class="headerlink" href="#operator" title="Link to this heading"></a></h4>
<p>What operation should be performed. ‘==’, ‘&gt;’,’&lt;’ etc.
Some operators require a target, to be compared with, others simply work on the source.</p>
</section>
<section id="target">
<h4>Target<a class="headerlink" href="#target" title="Link to this heading"></a></h4>
<p>If the operator requires a second value to compare with, a target needs to be specified.</p>
</section>
<section id="output">
<h4>Output<a class="headerlink" href="#output" title="Link to this heading"></a></h4>
<p>On a successful match, the output for the comparison is returned.</p>
</section>
</section>
</section>
<section id="default-config">
<h2>Default config<a class="headerlink" href="#default-config" title="Link to this heading"></a></h2>
<p>Under the hood, this is the default block config JSON.</p>
<div class="highlight-json notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><span class="p">{</span>
<span class="w">  </span><span class="kc">t</span><span class="err">ype</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="err">&#39;compariso</span><span class="kc">n</span><span class="err">&#39;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">  </span><span class="err">valueGe</span><span class="kc">tter</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="err">&#39;da</span><span class="kc">ta</span><span class="err">&#39;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">  </span><span class="err">de</span><span class="kc">fault</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="err">&#39;NO&#39;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">  </span><span class="err">de</span><span class="kc">fault</span><span class="err">Type</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="err">&#39;value&#39;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">  </span><span class="err">compariso</span><span class="kc">ns</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="p">[</span>
<span class="w">    </span><span class="p">{</span>
<span class="w">      </span><span class="err">opera</span><span class="kc">t</span><span class="err">or</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="err">&#39;==&#39;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">      </span><span class="kc">tar</span><span class="err">ge</span><span class="kc">t</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="err">&#39;OK&#39;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">      </span><span class="kc">tar</span><span class="err">ge</span><span class="kc">t</span><span class="err">Type</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="err">&#39;value&#39;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">      </span><span class="err">ou</span><span class="kc">t</span><span class="err">pu</span><span class="kc">t</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="err">&#39;YES&#39;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">      </span><span class="err">ou</span><span class="kc">t</span><span class="err">pu</span><span class="kc">t</span><span class="err">Type</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="err">&#39;value&#39;</span>
<span class="w">    </span><span class="p">}</span>
<span class="w">  </span><span class="p">]</span>
<span class="p">}</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
</section>
</section>

<li class="toctree-l1"><a class="reference internal" href="../../testing/tips.html">Notes for Cypress testing</a></li>
</ul>

        </div>
      </div>
    </nav>

    <section data-toggle="wy-nav-shift" class="wy-nav-content-wrap"><nav class="wy-nav-top" aria-label="Mobile navigation menu" >
          <i data-toggle="wy-nav-top" class="fa fa-bars"></i>
          <a href="../../index.html">Kendraio App</a>
      </nav>

      <div class="wy-nav-content">
        <div class="rst-content style-external-links">
          <div role="navigation" aria-label="Page navigation">
  <ul class="wy-breadcrumbs">
      <li><a href="../../index.html" class="icon icon-home" aria-label="Home"></a></li>
      <li class="breadcrumb-item active">Template</li>
      <li class="wy-breadcrumbs-aside">
            <a href="../../_sources/workflow/blocks/template.rst.txt" rel="nofollow"> View page source</a>
      </li>
  </ul>
  <hr/>
</div>
          <div role="main" class="document" itemscope="itemscope" itemtype="http://schema.org/Article">
           <div itemprop="articleBody">
             
  <section id="template">
<h1>Template<a class="headerlink" href="#template" title="Link to this heading"></a></h1>
<p>Render templated output based on current model values. Rendered HTML also will be added to the data model to be accessible in the flow.</p>
<p>All data is passed through a template block unmodified, with the rendered content available at “data.renderedHtml”.</p>
<section id="default-config">
<h2>Default config<a class="headerlink" href="#default-config" title="Link to this heading"></a></h2>
<div class="highlight-json notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><span class="p">{</span>
<span class="w">  </span><span class="nt">&quot;type&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;template&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">  </span><span class="nt">&quot;template&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;&quot;</span>
<span class="p">}</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
</section>
<section id="supported-properties">
<h2>Supported properties<a class="headerlink" href="#supported-properties" title="Link to this heading"></a></h2>
<ul class="simple">
<li><p><strong>renderToScreen</strong> - (default: true) Display rendered HTML in the browser. Set to false to hide rendered html</p></li>
<li><p><strong>key</strong> - (default: “renderedHtml”) Name of the data model property to store rendered HTML in.</p></li>
</ul>
</section>
<section id="more-information">
<h2>More information<a class="headerlink" href="#more-information" title="Link to this heading"></a></h2>
<p>See the Handlebars documentation for full list of supported templating features.</p>
<p><a class="reference external" href="https://handlebarsjs.com/">https://handlebarsjs.com/</a></p>
</section>
</section>


           </div>
          </div>
          <footer><div class="rst-footer-buttons" role="navigation" aria-label="Footer">

          <i data-toggle="wy-nav-top" class="fa fa-bars"></i>
          <a href="../../index.html">Kendraio App</a>
      </nav>

      <div class="wy-nav-content">
        <div class="rst-content style-external-links">
          <div role="navigation" aria-label="Page navigation">
  <ul class="wy-breadcrumbs">
      <li><a href="../../index.html" class="icon icon-home" aria-label="Home"></a></li>
      <li class="breadcrumb-item active">Dialog</li>
      <li class="wy-breadcrumbs-aside">
            <a href="../../_sources/workflow/blocks/dialog.rst.txt" rel="nofollow"> View page source</a>
      </li>
  </ul>
  <hr/>
</div>
          <div role="main" class="document" itemscope="itemscope" itemtype="http://schema.org/Article">
           <div itemprop="articleBody">
             
  <section id="dialog">
<h1>Dialog<a class="headerlink" href="#dialog" title="Link to this heading"></a></h1>
<p>Launch a modal dialog in which to run an embedded workflow.</p>
<section id="default-config">
<h2>Default config<a class="headerlink" href="#default-config" title="Link to this heading"></a></h2>
<div class="highlight-json notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><span class="p">{</span>
<span class="w">  </span><span class="nt">&quot;type&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;dialog&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">  </span><span class="nt">&quot;blocks&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="p">[]</span>
<span class="p">}</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
<p><strong>NB:</strong> The modal dialog will close as soon as the inner workflow completes.
Therefore you probably want to include at least one workflow item that requires interaction
from the user, for example, <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">actions</span></code> buttons or a <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">form</span></code>.</p>
</section>
<section id="example">
<h2>Example<a class="headerlink" href="#example" title="Link to this heading"></a></h2>
<p>The following example is used within the blocks of a button to launch a confirmation
dialog with two options, OK, and Cancel.</p>
<div class="highlight-json notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><span class="p">{</span>
<span class="w">  </span><span class="nt">&quot;type&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;dialog&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">  </span><span class="nt">&quot;blocks&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="p">[</span>
<span class="w">    </span><span class="p">{</span>
<span class="w">      </span><span class="nt">&quot;type&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;message&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">      </span><span class="nt">&quot;title&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;Are you sure you want to reset the app?&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">      </span><span class="nt">&quot;message&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;This action will remove all data and settings.&quot;</span>
<span class="w">    </span><span class="p">},</span>
<span class="w">    </span><span class="p">{</span>
<span class="w">      </span><span class="nt">&quot;type&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;actions&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">      </span><span class="nt">&quot;buttons&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="p">[</span>
<span class="w">        </span><span class="p">{</span>
<span class="w">          </span><span class="nt">&quot;label&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;OK&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">          </span><span class="nt">&quot;color&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;primary&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">          </span><span class="nt">&quot;blocks&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="p">[</span>
<span class="w">            </span><span class="p">{</span>
<span class="w">              </span><span class="nt">&quot;type&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;dispatch&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">              </span><span class="nt">&quot;action&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;resetApp&quot;</span>
<span class="w">            </span><span class="p">}</span>
<span class="w">          </span><span class="p">]</span>
<span class="w">        </span><span class="p">},</span>
<span class="w">        </span><span class="p">{</span>
<span class="w">          </span><span class="nt">&quot;label&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;Cancel&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">          </span><span class="nt">&quot;blocks&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="p">[</span>
<span class="w">            </span><span class="p">{</span>
<span class="w">              </span><span class="nt">&quot;type&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;init&quot;</span>
<span class="w">            </span><span class="p">}</span>
<span class="w">          </span><span class="p">]</span>
<span class="w">        </span><span class="p">}</span>
<span class="w">      </span><span class="p">]</span>
<span class="w">    </span><span class="p">}</span>
<span class="w">  </span><span class="p">]</span>
<span class="p">}</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
</section>
</section>


           </div>
          </div>

  <ul class="wy-breadcrumbs">
      <li><a href="../../index.html" class="icon icon-home" aria-label="Home"></a></li>

          <div role="main" class="document" itemscope="itemscope" itemtype="http://schema.org/Article">
           <div itemprop="articleBody">
             
  <section id="local-database">
<h1>Local Database<a class="headerlink" href="#local-database" title="Link to this heading"></a></h1>
<p>The local database block implements an indexed database in the browser storage.
The block implements a version of Dexie.js.</p>
<p>All data stored using this block is written to the same local database.
To manage subsets of the database, we segment data based on the combination
of “adapterName” and “schemaName”.  Individual items can be loaded with just a
uuid, while dataset of items can be loaded by providing schemaName and adapterName.</p>
<p>For more information about how data is stored in the local database, see the section at the end of this document.</p>
<section id="default-config">
<h2>Default config<a class="headerlink" href="#default-config" title="Link to this heading"></a></h2>
<div class="highlight-javascript notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><span class="p">{</span>
<span class="w">  </span><span class="nx">type</span><span class="o">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s1">&#39;db&#39;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">  </span><span class="nx">operation</span><span class="o">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s1">&#39;add&#39;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">  </span><span class="nx">adapterName</span><span class="o">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s1">&#39;UNKNOWN&#39;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">  </span><span class="nx">schema</span><span class="o">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s1">&#39;none&#39;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">  </span><span class="nx">skipFirst</span><span class="o">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="kc">true</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="p">}</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
</section>
<section id="available-operations">
<h2>Available operations<a class="headerlink" href="#available-operations" title="Link to this heading"></a></h2>
<ul class="simple">
<li><p><strong>fetch</strong> - load a single item from the database</p></li>
<li><p><strong>get</strong> - load multiple items from the database</p></li>
<li><p><strong>update</strong> - modify a single item in the database</p></li>
<li><p><strong>add</strong> - add a new item</p></li>
<li><p><strong>delete</strong> - delete an item</p></li>
<li><p><strong>upsert</strong> - adds or updates an item. (Must have a UUID specified in the data)</p></li>
</ul>
</section>
<section id="supported-properties">
<h2>Supported properties<a class="headerlink" href="#supported-properties" title="Link to this heading"></a></h2>
<ul class="simple">
<li><p><strong>operation</strong> -  the database operation to undertake (required)</p>
<ul>
<li><p>for <strong>fetch</strong> or <strong>delete</strong>
<strong>uuidGetter</strong>  A JMESPath expression to resolve the uuid of the item.</p></li>
<li><p>for <strong>add</strong> or <strong>get</strong>
<strong>adapterName</strong>  - The name of the adapter to use (required)
<strong>schema</strong> -  the name of the schema
<strong>schemaGetter</strong> - A JMESPath expression to resolve the schema name</p></li>
<li><p>for <strong>update</strong> there are no properties required, simply the data</p></li>
</ul>
</li>
<li><p><strong>skipFirst</strong> - (defaults = true) don’t act during the block’s initialisation phase</p></li>
</ul>
</section>
<section id="localdatabaseservice">
<h2>LocalDatabaseService<a class="headerlink" href="#localdatabaseservice" title="Link to this heading"></a></h2>
<p>The local database block builds on the local database service. Our local database service is a wrapper for Dexie.js.
The LocalDatabaseService defines a set of database tables, but, when we’re working with the bd block, all our data
if stored in a single table - “metadata”.</p>
<p>Internally, all data in the “metadata” table uses this format:</p>
<div class="highlight-json notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><span class="p">{</span>
<span class="w">    </span><span class="nt">&quot;uuid&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">    </span><span class="nt">&quot;schemaName&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">    </span><span class="nt">&quot;adapterName&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">    </span><span class="nt">&quot;label&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">    </span><span class="nt">&quot;data&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="p">{</span>
<span class="w">        </span><span class="nt">&quot;key1&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;value&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">        </span><span class="nt">&quot;key2&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;value&quot;</span>
<span class="w">    </span><span class="p">}</span>
<span class="p">}</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
<p>Individual items can be loaded with just a uuid, while collections of items can be loaded by providing schemaName and adapterName.</p>
</section>
</section>

          <div role="main" class="document" itemscope="itemscope" itemtype="http://schema.org/Article">
           <div itemprop="articleBody">
             
  <section id="app-layout">
<h1>App Layout<a class="headerlink" href="#app-layout" title="Link to this heading"></a></h1>
<p>A flow can be set to use a simplified layout for display on mobile devices.
To access this functionality a flow is tagged with the keyword: “app”.</p>
<p>The app-layout block provides a fixed navigation bar at the bottom of the
display that works well on mobile devices. The navigation items are specified
with an icon, title, and list of blocks.</p>
<p>It is recommended that the screens should be created as separate flows
and them embedded using the gosub block. Unless they are very simple flows,
in which case they can be inserted directly into the app-layout block config.</p>
<p>The icon field accepts any valid Material Icon. The full list of possible icons can be
searched here: <a class="reference external" href="https://fonts.google.com/icons">https://fonts.google.com/icons</a></p>
<div class="highlight-json notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><span class="p">{</span>
<span class="w">    </span><span class="nt">&quot;type&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;app-layout&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">    </span><span class="nt">&quot;tabs&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="p">[</span>
<span class="w">        </span><span class="p">{</span>
<span class="w">            </span><span class="nt">&quot;icon&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;face&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">            </span><span class="nt">&quot;title&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;Account&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">            </span><span class="nt">&quot;blocks&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="p">[</span>
<span class="w">                </span><span class="p">{</span>
<span class="w">                    </span><span class="nt">&quot;type&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;message&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">                    </span><span class="nt">&quot;message&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;Account Tab&quot;</span>
<span class="w">                </span><span class="p">}</span>
<span class="w">            </span><span class="p">]</span>
<span class="w">        </span><span class="p">},</span>
<span class="w">        </span><span class="p">{</span>
<span class="w">            </span><span class="nt">&quot;icon&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;stacked_bar_chart&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">            </span><span class="nt">&quot;title&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;Chart&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">            </span><span class="nt">&quot;blocks&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="p">[</span>
<span class="w">                </span><span class="p">{</span>
<span class="w">                    </span><span class="nt">&quot;type&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;gosub&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">                    </span><span class="nt">&quot;adapterName&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;kendraio&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">                    </span><span class="nt">&quot;workflowId&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;coronavirusByCountry&quot;</span>
<span class="w">                </span><span class="p">}</span>
<span class="w">            </span><span class="p">]</span>
<span class="w">        </span><span class="p">},</span>
<span class="w">        </span><span class="p">{</span>
<span class="w">            </span><span class="nt">&quot;icon&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;settings&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">            </span><span class="nt">&quot;title&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;Settings&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">            </span><span class="nt">&quot;blocks&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="p">[</span>
<span class="w">                </span><span class="p">{</span>
<span class="w">                    </span><span class="nt">&quot;type&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;message&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">                    </span><span class="nt">&quot;message&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;Settings Tab&quot;</span>
<span class="w">                </span><span class="p">}</span>
<span class="w">            </span><span class="p">]</span>
<span class="w">        </span><span class="p">}</span>
<span class="w">    </span><span class="p">]</span>
<span class="p">}</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
</section>

</div>
          <div role="main" class="document" itemscope="itemscope" itemtype="http://schema.org/Article">
           <div itemprop="articleBody">
             
  <section id="serialize-data">
<h1>Serialize Data<a class="headerlink" href="#serialize-data" title="Link to this heading"></a></h1>
<p>This block will convert the incoming data into an encoded/serialized string of text data. The encoding of the data depends on the configuration and can be selectable by the user.</p>
<p>The output of this block is an object with two keys:</p>
<ul class="simple">
<li><p><strong>format</strong> (default = ‘json’) - a string representing the type of the serialized data</p></li>
<li><p><strong>data</strong> - the text string with the encoded data</p></li>
</ul>
<section id="default-config">
<h2>Default config<a class="headerlink" href="#default-config" title="Link to this heading"></a></h2>
<div class="highlight-json notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><span class="p">{</span>
<span class="w">  </span><span class="nt">&quot;type&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;serialize&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">  </span><span class="nt">&quot;hasFormatSelection&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="kc">true</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">  </span><span class="nt">&quot;formats&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="p">[</span>
<span class="w">    </span><span class="s2">&quot;json&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">    </span><span class="s2">&quot;csv&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">    </span><span class="s2">&quot;xml&quot;</span>
<span class="w">  </span><span class="p">],</span>
<span class="w">  </span><span class="nt">&quot;csvOptions&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="p">{</span>
<span class="w">    </span><span class="nt">&quot;header&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="kc">true</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">    </span><span class="nt">&quot;skipEmptyLines&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="kc">true</span>
<span class="w">  </span><span class="p">},</span>
<span class="w">  </span><span class="nt">&quot;xmlOptions&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="p">{</span>

<span class="w">  </span><span class="p">}</span>
<span class="p">}</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
</section>
<section id="supported-properties">
<h2>Supported properties<a class="headerlink" href="#supported-properties" title="Link to this heading"></a></h2>
<ul class="simple">
<li><p><strong>hasFormatSelection</strong> (default = true) - show a widget to select the output format.</p></li>
<li><p><strong>formats</strong> (default = [‘json’, ‘csv’, ‘xml’]) - which formats to allow for output</p></li>
<li><p><strong>showRecordCount</strong> (default = true) set this to false to disable the display of the record counter</p></li>
<li><p><strong>csvOptions</strong> - configuration options to pass along to CSV serializer</p></li>
<li><p><strong>xmlOptions</strong> - configuration options to pass along to the XML serializer</p></li>
</ul>
<p>For CSV data the input data can be one of:</p>
<ul class="simple">
<li><p>An array of arrays</p></li>
<li><p>An array of objects</p></li>
<li><p>An object explicitly defining fields and data</p></li>
</ul>
<p>Supported <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">csvOptions</span></code> with default values:</p>
<ul class="simple">
<li><p><strong>quotes</strong>: false (or array of booleans</p></li>
<li><p><strong>quoteChar</strong>: ‘”’</p></li>
<li><p><strong>escapeChar</strong>: ‘”’</p></li>
<li><p><strong>delimiter</strong>: “,”</p></li>
<li><p><strong>header</strong>: true</p></li>
<li><p><strong>newline</strong>: “rn”</p></li>
<li><p><strong>skipEmptyLines</strong>: false (or ‘greedy’)</p></li>
<li><p><strong>columns</strong>: null (or array of strings)</p></li>
</ul>
</section>
</section>

</ul>
</li>

<li class="toctree-l1"><a class="reference internal" href="wallet.html">Wallet</a></li>
</ul>

  <hr/>
</div>
          <div role="main" class="document" itemscope="itemscope" itemtype="http://schema.org/Article">
           <div itemprop="articleBody">
             
  <section id="query">
<h1>Query<a class="headerlink" href="#query" title="Link to this heading"></a></h1>
<p>Run a configurable query against a data source</p>
<p><strong>NB:</strong> Use of this block for HTTP calls is deprecated, and you should use the dedicated HTTP block. This block will
be focused on making calls to the local DB</p>
<section id="default-config">
<h2>Default config<a class="headerlink" href="#default-config" title="Link to this heading"></a></h2>
<div class="highlight-json notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><span class="p">{</span>
<span class="w">  </span><span class="nt">&quot;type&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;query&quot;</span>
<span class="p">}</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
</section>
</section>

  <ul class="wy-breadcrumbs">
      <li><a href="../../index.html" class="icon icon-home" aria-label="Home"></a></li>
      <li class="breadcrumb-item active">Faker</li>

  <hr/>
</div>
          <div role="main" class="document" itemscope="itemscope" itemtype="http://schema.org/Article">
           <div itemprop="articleBody">
             
  <section id="faker">
<h1>Faker<a class="headerlink" href="#faker" title="Link to this heading"></a></h1>
<p>Generate fake data for testing purposes.</p>
<section id="default-config">
<h2>Default config<a class="headerlink" href="#default-config" title="Link to this heading"></a></h2>
<div class="highlight-json notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><span class="p">{</span>
<span class="w">  </span><span class="nt">&quot;type&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;faker&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">  </span><span class="nt">&quot;jsonSchema&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="p">{}</span>
<span class="p">}</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
</section>
<section id="supported-properties">
<h2>Supported properties<a class="headerlink" href="#supported-properties" title="Link to this heading"></a></h2>
<ul class="simple">
<li><p><strong>jsonSchema</strong> - The schema for the generated fake data. For full details of supported features see: <a class="reference external" href="https://github.com/json-schema-faker/json-schema-faker/blob/master/docs/USAGE.md">https://github.com/json-schema-faker/json-schema-faker/blob/master/docs/USAGE.md</a></p></li>
</ul>
<p>The following properties from JSON Schema are supported by the fake data generator:</p>
<ul class="simple">
<li><p><strong>$ref</strong> — Resolve internal references only, and/or external if provided.</p></li>
<li><p><strong>required</strong> — All required properties are guaranteed, if not can be omitted.</p></li>
<li><p><strong>pattern</strong> — Generate samples based on RegExp values.</p></li>
<li><p><strong>format</strong> — Core formats v4-draft only: date-time, email, hostname, ipv4, ipv6 and uri - also uri-reference, uri-template, json-pointer and uuid are supported.</p></li>
<li><p><strong>enum</strong> — Returns any of these enumerated values.</p></li>
<li><p><strong>minLength</strong>, <strong>maxLength</strong> — Applies length constraints to string values.</p></li>
<li><p><strong>minimum</strong>, <strong>maximum</strong> — Applies constraints to numeric values.</p></li>
<li><p><strong>exclusiveMinimum</strong>, <strong>exclusiveMaximum</strong> — Adds exclusivity for numeric values.</p></li>
<li><p><strong>multipleOf</strong> — Multiple constraints for numeric values.</p></li>
<li><p><strong>items</strong> — Support for subschema and fixed item values.</p></li>
<li><p><strong>minItems</strong>, maxItems — Adds length constraints for array items.</p></li>
<li><p><strong>uniqueItems</strong> — Applies uniqueness constraints for array items.</p></li>
<li><p><strong>additionalItems</strong> — Partially supported (?)</p></li>
<li><p><strong>allOf</strong>, <strong>oneOf</strong>, <strong>anyOf</strong> — Subschema combinators.</p></li>
<li><p><strong>properties</strong> — Object properties to be generated.</p></li>
<li><p><strong>minProperties</strong>, <strong>maxProperties</strong> — Adds length constraints for object properties.</p></li>
<li><p><strong>patternProperties</strong> — RegExp-based object properties.</p></li>
<li><p><strong>additionalProperties</strong> — Partially supported (?)</p></li>
<li><p><strong>dependencies</strong> — Partially supported (?)</p></li>
<li><p><strong>not</strong> — Not supported yet (?)</p></li>
</ul>
<p>JSON schema faker includes basic support for generating values, but more advanced generators are available by using the “faker” extension.
All fake data generators from the faker.js package can be used, see <a class="reference external" href="https://github.com/marak/Faker.js/">https://github.com/marak/Faker.js/</a> for full list of available data generators.
To specify a faker generator, add the “faker” key to the JSON Schema, for example:</p>
<div class="highlight-json notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><span class="p">{</span>
<span class="w">  </span><span class="nt">&quot;type&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;string&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">  </span><span class="nt">&quot;faker&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;internet.email&quot;</span>
<span class="p">}</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
</section>
</section>

  <hr/>
</div>
          <div role="main" class="document" itemscope="itemscope" itemtype="http://schema.org/Article">
           <div itemprop="articleBody">
             
  <section id="event-dispatch">
<h1>Event Dispatch<a class="headerlink" href="#event-dispatch" title="Link to this heading"></a></h1>
<p>Dispatch an event to trigger asynchronous processing.</p>
<section id="default-config">
<h2>Default config<a class="headerlink" href="#default-config" title="Link to this heading"></a></h2>
<div class="highlight-json notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><span class="p">{</span>
<span class="w">  </span><span class="nt">&quot;type&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;dispatch&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">  </span><span class="nt">&quot;action&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;[TASK_NAME]&quot;</span>
<span class="p">}</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
</section>
<section id="usage">
<h2>Usage<a class="headerlink" href="#usage" title="Link to this heading"></a></h2>
<p>This block is only really useful by core workflows, as adapter workflows
are not yet able to register asynchronous workflows to run in response to events.</p>
<p>To track development of this feature, see this issue:
<a class="reference external" href="https://github.com/kendraio/kendraio-app/issues/53">https://github.com/kendraio/kendraio-app/issues/53</a></p>
</section>
</section>

  <hr/>
</div>
          <div role="main" class="document" itemscope="itemscope" itemtype="http://schema.org/Article">
           <div itemprop="articleBody">
             
  <section id="gosub">
<h1>Gosub<a class="headerlink" href="#gosub" title="Link to this heading"></a></h1>
<p>Embedded a workflow within a workflow.</p>
<p>This allows composition of larger building blocks to create workflows that
are easier to manage, and DRY (don’t repeat yourself).</p>
<p>Typical use-cases for this are creating custom form widgets that can
be referenced from a form’s <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">uiSchema</span></code>. Or, creating dashboards
that are made up of multiple other workflows combined with a <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">multi</span></code>
multiplex workflow block.</p>
</section>


           </div>
          </div>
          <footer><div class="rst-footer-buttons" role="navigation" aria-label="Footer">

        <a href="grid.html" class="btn btn-neutral float-right" title="Data Grid" accesskey="n" rel="next">Next <span class="fa fa-arrow-circle-right" aria-hidden="true"></span></a>
    </div>

      </li>

  <hr/>
</div>
          <div role="main" class="document" itemscope="itemscope" itemtype="http://schema.org/Article">
           <div itemprop="articleBody">
             
  <section id="google-sheet">
<h1>Google Sheet<a class="headerlink" href="#google-sheet" title="Link to this heading"></a></h1>
<p>Use a Google Spreadsheet as a data source.</p>
<p>First, publishing your Google Sheet: In the File menu pick Share and then Publish to the web.
Select the sheet you want to access, choose CSV as the format, and then click “Publish”</p>
<p>Copy the published doc link.</p>
<p>Enter the URL from above as the key option in the block config:</p>
<div class="highlight-json notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><span class="p">{</span>
<span class="w">    </span><span class="nt">&quot;type&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;gsheet&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">    </span><span class="nt">&quot;shareUrl&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/e/2PACX-1vTk4ki2qQI9vuhPEH_QLZS-SYktDZke7dJkt_x7qTxicoPlepm94cCrRPP5Bi2r9oyMEtFFXKUQLtpV/pub?gid=0&amp;single=true&amp;output=csv&quot;</span>
<span class="p">}</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
</section>


           </div>
          </div>
          <footer><div class="rst-footer-buttons" role="navigation" aria-label="Footer">

          <div role="main" class="document" itemscope="itemscope" itemtype="http://schema.org/Article">
           <div itemprop="articleBody">
             
  <section id="file-input">
<h1>File Input<a class="headerlink" href="#file-input" title="Link to this heading"></a></h1>
<p>Import a file from the local system.</p>
<section id="default-config">
<h2>Default config<a class="headerlink" href="#default-config" title="Link to this heading"></a></h2>
<div class="highlight-json notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><span class="p">{</span>
<span class="w">  </span><span class="nt">&quot;type&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;file-input&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">  </span><span class="nt">&quot;label&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;Import&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">  </span><span class="nt">&quot;accept&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="p">[</span><span class="s2">&quot;csv&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;json&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;xml&quot;</span><span class="p">]</span>
<span class="p">}</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
</section>
<section id="supported-properties">
<h2>Supported properties<a class="headerlink" href="#supported-properties" title="Link to this heading"></a></h2>
<ul class="simple">
<li><p><strong>accept</strong> (default = [‘json’]) a list of formats to allow for import. The file selection will limit the selectable files to only these types. Supported types include: [‘json’, ‘csv’, ‘xml’]</p></li>
<li><p><strong>label</strong> (default = “import”) - the label for the button to launch the file picker.</p></li>
</ul>
</section>
<section id="emitted-data">
<h2>Emitted data<a class="headerlink" href="#emitted-data" title="Link to this heading"></a></h2>
<p>The data emitted from this block has the following format:</p>
<ul class="simple">
<li><p><strong>name</strong> - name of file that was selected</p></li>
<li><p><strong>type</strong> - the MIME type of the file</p></li>
<li><p><strong>size</strong> - the size in bytes of the selected file</p></li>
<li><p><strong>lastModified</strong> - the last modified timestamp</p></li>
<li><p><strong>content</strong> - a text string containing the full content of the file</p></li>
</ul>
</section>
</section>


           </div>
          </div>

    <section data-toggle="wy-nav-shift" class="wy-nav-content-wrap"><nav class="wy-nav-top" aria-label="Mobile navigation menu" >

          <div role="navigation" aria-label="Page navigation">
  <ul class="wy-breadcrumbs">
      <li><a href="../../index.html" class="icon icon-home" aria-label="Home"></a></li>
      <li class="breadcrumb-item active">Context Runner Block</li>
      <li class="wy-breadcrumbs-aside">
