           <div itemprop="articleBody">
             
  <section id="mapping">
<h1>Mapping<a class="headerlink" href="#mapping" title="Link to this heading"></a></h1>
<p>Map data from one format to another, or query the input data using JMESPath or JSONPath expressions.
<a class="reference external" href="http://jmespath.org/tutorial.html">http://jmespath.org/tutorial.html</a>.</p>
<p>The mapping object has three top level keys
- data : The core data being processed and passed from block to block
- context : Metadata about the data being processed, also passed from block to block
- state : General metadata about the general state of processing, shared by all blocks</p>
<section id="default-config">
<h2>Default config<a class="headerlink" href="#default-config" title="Link to this heading"></a></h2>
<div class="highlight-json notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><span class="p">{</span>
<span class="w">  </span><span class="nt">&quot;type&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;mapping&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">  </span><span class="nt">&quot;mapping&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;data&quot;</span>
<span class="p">}</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
</section>
<section id="supported-properties">
<h2>Supported properties<a class="headerlink" href="#supported-properties" title="Link to this heading"></a></h2>
<ul class="simple">
<li><p><strong>mapping</strong> - a JMESPath expression.</p></li>
</ul>
</section>
<section id="working-with-state">
<h2>Working with State<a class="headerlink" href="#working-with-state" title="Link to this heading"></a></h2>
<p>State is a shared, realtime storage. It is also available between flows but will not persist beyond a single user session.
Not all blocks are able to access state, but it is particularly useful for user interface components that want to keep updating
their display to show the user live values - rather than the value that was passed into the block when it was run.</p>
<p>The mapping block cannot write to state, it can only read from state. Writes are managed with the <a class="reference internal" href="context_block.html"><span class="doc">Context &amp; State</span></a> block.</p>
<section id="local-and-global-state">
<h3>Local and global State<a class="headerlink" href="#local-and-global-state" title="Link to this heading"></a></h3>
<p>It is assumed that the state storage will mirror the site structure. Flows should generally read from <em>state.local</em>.
Internally <em>state.local</em> will be translated to a path to mirror the current url.</p>
<p>When on a the path <em>/workflowCloud/listWorkflows</em>, reading from <em>state.local.filter</em> will be translated to read from  <em>workflowCloud.listWorkflows.filter</em>.
This internal, translated path is the global state path, and it can also be accessed directly via <em>state.global</em>.</p>
<p>When a path is prefixed with <em>state.global</em>, it will allow values to be read from to any part of the tree. Reading from <em>state.global.filter</em> will be translated to
read from <em>filter</em>. Reading from  <em>state.global.workflowCloud.listWorkflows.filter</em> will access <em>workflowCloud.listWorkflows.filter</em>.</p>
</section>
</section>
<section id="handy-jmespath-patterns">
<h2>Handy JMESPath patterns<a class="headerlink" href="#handy-jmespath-patterns" title="Link to this heading"></a></h2>
</section>
<section id="using-a-string-as-a-value">
<h2>Using a string as a value<a class="headerlink" href="#using-a-string-as-a-value" title="Link to this heading"></a></h2>
<p>To be valid JMESPath, keys and values must be wrapped in double quotes.
A hardcoded string’s value must be wrapped in backticks, otherwise it will result in <cite>null</cite> for undefined variables.</p>
<div class="highlight-json notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><span class="p">{</span>
<span class="w">   </span><span class="nt">&quot;name&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="err">`Joh</span><span class="kc">n</span><span class="err">`</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">   </span><span class="nt">&quot;surname&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="err">`Doe`</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">   </span><span class="nt">&quot;iAmNull&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;not showing&quot;</span>
<span class="p">}</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
<p>Output will be:</p>
<div class="highlight-text notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span>name: &quot;John&quot;
surname: &quot;Doe&quot;
iAmNull: null
</pre></div>
</div>
<p>It is also possible to wrap a whole JSON object in backticks:</p>
<div class="highlight-json notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><span class="err">`</span><span class="p">{</span>
<span class="w">   </span><span class="nt">&quot;name&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;John&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">   </span><span class="nt">&quot;surname&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;Doe&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">   </span><span class="nt">&quot;iAmNotNull&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;now this value is visible too&quot;</span>
<span class="p">}</span><span class="err">`</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
</section>
<section id="not">
<h2>Not<a class="headerlink" href="#not" title="Link to this heading"></a></h2>
<p>When your data structuture holds the value that you wish to negate, you need to enclose the
path of your data in parentheses before you NOT it.</p>
<p><strong>This statement will fail</strong></p>
<div class="highlight-text notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span>!state.import.status
</pre></div>
</div>
<p><strong>Use this instead</strong></p>
<div class="highlight-text notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span>!(state.import.status)
</pre></div>
</div>
<p>If you want to combine a NOT statement like this with additional logic, you need to wrap it in parentheses again.</p>
<div class="highlight-text notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span>(!(state.import.status)) &amp;&amp; (length(state.import.data)&gt;`0`)
</pre></div>
</div>
</section>
<section id="default-values">
<h2>Default values<a class="headerlink" href="#default-values" title="Link to this heading"></a></h2>
<p>Set default values by using “||” (or)</p>
<div class="highlight-text notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span>data || {}
</pre></div>
</div>
</section>
<section id="filtering-data">
<h2>Filtering data<a class="headerlink" href="#filtering-data" title="Link to this heading"></a></h2>
<p>Filter by the existence of a flag</p>
<div class="highlight-text notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span>data[?tags[?@ == &#39;deleted&#39;]]
</pre></div>
</div>
<p>This will keep any item that has a “deleted” flag in the “tags” array. More useful in this instance would be it’s inverse.</p>
<div class="highlight-text notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span>data[?!(tags[?@ == &#39;deleted&#39;])]
</pre></div>
</div>
<p>If you’re wrangling data from one form to another, you may need to add new keys.</p>
<div class="highlight-text notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span>data.merge(@, { key: value })
</pre></div>
</div>
<p>If your data is an array, you can iterate through its elements with <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">[*]</span></code>.
In the below example, the new key/value pair is added to each element of the array.</p>
<div class="highlight-text notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span>data[*].merge(@, { key: value })
</pre></div>
</div>
<p>If you want to add a new value to your object based on whether or not another flag is set in an array, you can do something like this.</p>
<div class="highlight-text notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span>data[*].merge(@,{deleted: @.tags[?@ == `deleted`]})
</pre></div>
</div>
<p>This will look through the tags attribute to see whether or not a “deleted” tag is set, and set the new attribute accordingly.</p>
<p>When using a merge, or similar function, paths become relative to your current item.
If you need to access a value from beyond this context, you need to use the “$” operator to access the root of the data.</p>
<div class="highlight-text notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span>data[*].merge(@,{
    exists:contains($.context.flowsExisting || [&#39;&#39;], join(&#39;-&#39;,[@.adapterName,@.workflowId]))
    })
</pre></div>
</div>
<p>A more advanced version of the object merge can involve looking up a specific entry in an existing array of objects.</p>
<p>In this example, we merge two object arrays, and look up a unique key from a list, and then add that key so that we can create a reference.</p>
<div class="highlight-text notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span>data &amp;&amp; merge(data, {track: uniqBy(data.track, &#39;key&#39;)})
</pre></div>
</div>
<div class="highlight-text notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span>find($.data.licensor, &#39;name&#39;, &quot;LICENSOR NAME&quot;).id,
</pre></div>
</div>
<p>This example looks through an array of objects, extracts the “name”, at the top level
and then pulls out all the “config” values from an array of properties</p>
<div class="highlight-text notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span>data[*].[name, @.properties[*].[config][] ]
</pre></div>
</div>
<p>This can also filter content based on properties</p>
<div class="highlight-text notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span>data[*].[name, properties[?type==`Object` || type==`List` || type==`ObjectReference` || type==`ListReference`].[config][]]
</pre></div>
</div>
<p>And this version will return an array of objects</p>
<div class="highlight-text notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span>data[*].{parent: name, children: properties[?type==`Object` || type==`List` || type==`ObjectReference` || type==`ListReference`].[config][]}
</pre></div>
</div>
</section>
<section id="merging-two-arrays">
<h2>Merging two arrays<a class="headerlink" href="#merging-two-arrays" title="Link to this heading"></a></h2>
<p>If you have two arrays, and want to combine them, you can use the flatten operator “[]”.
For example, if you want to combine information from a form with data you’ve previously saved to context, you can merge the two arrays like this.</p>
<div class="highlight-text notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span>[context.saved, [data]][]
</pre></div>
</div>
</section>
<section id="creating-an-object-from-two-arrays-spreadsheet-import">
<h2>Creating an object from two arrays - spreadsheet import<a class="headerlink" href="#creating-an-object-from-two-arrays-spreadsheet-import" title="Link to this heading"></a></h2>
<p>To import from a spreadsheet, converting each row into an object with keys in the first row,
you need a combination of actions.</p>
<div class="highlight-text notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span>data &amp;&amp; data.Sheet1[1:].[$.data.Sheet1[0], @].map(&amp;fromPairs(zip([0], [1])), @)
</pre></div>
</div>
<ul class="simple">
<li><p>‘data.Sheet1[1:]’  skips the first row, which is the header row</p></li>
<li><p>‘[$.data.Sheet1[0], &#64;]’  for each row, take the first row and the current row</p></li>
<li><p>‘map([expression],[elements])’ apply an expression to every row, passing in the current row (&#64;)</p></li>
<li><p>‘&amp;fromPairs(..)’  convert an array of arrays into an object</p></li>
<li><dl class="simple">
<dt>‘zip([0], [1])’ zip the first row and the current row together,</dt><dd><p>creating an array of arrays with each column converted to [key, value] arrays</p>
</dd>
</dl>
</li>
</ul>
</section>
<section id="kendraio-mapping-extensions">
<h2>Kendraio Mapping extensions<a class="headerlink" href="#kendraio-mapping-extensions" title="Link to this heading"></a></h2>
<p>Kendraio is using a version of JMESPath that supports extensions to provide additional functionality. You can find these in our repository:</p>
<p><a class="reference external" href="https://github.com/kendraio/kendraio-app/blob/develop/src/app/blocks/mapping-block/mapping-util.ts">https://github.com/kendraio/kendraio-app/blob/develop/src/app/blocks/mapping-block/mapping-util.ts</a></p>
<p>These extensions are a set of additional functions that complement the standard JMESPath functionality, providing more advanced data manipulation and transformation capabilities. Example data, which is common to multiple examples, is shown below:</p>
<div class="highlight-json notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><span class="p">{</span>
<span class="w">    </span><span class="nt">&quot;name&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;John Doe&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">    </span><span class="nt">&quot;age&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="mi">35</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">    </span><span class="nt">&quot;email&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;john.doe@example.com&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">    </span><span class="nt">&quot;tags&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="p">[</span><span class="s2">&quot;frontend&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;ui&quot;</span><span class="p">],</span>
<span class="w">    </span><span class="nt">&quot;projects&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="p">[{</span><span class="nt">&quot;id&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="mi">1</span><span class="p">,</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="nt">&quot;title&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;Project A&quot;</span><span class="p">},</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="p">{</span><span class="nt">&quot;id&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="mi">2</span><span class="p">,</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="nt">&quot;title&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;Project B&quot;</span><span class="p">}]</span>
<span class="p">}</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
</section>
<section id="available-extensions">
<h2>Available Extensions<a class="headerlink" href="#available-extensions" title="Link to this heading"></a></h2>
<ol class="arabic simple">
<li><p>get</p></li>
<li><p>set</p></li>
<li><p>findIndex</p></li>
<li><p>uuid</p></li>
<li><p>toLower</p></li>
<li><p>toUpper</p></li>
<li><p>replace</p></li>
<li><p>replaceAll</p></li>
<li><p>trim</p></li>
<li><p>now</p></li>
<li><p>formatDate</p></li>
<li><p>omit</p></li>
<li><p>pick</p></li>
<li><p>split</p></li>
<li><p>find</p></li>
<li><p>compact</p></li>
<li><p>qs</p></li>
<li><p>parseQs</p></li>
<li><p>zip</p></li>
<li><p>debug</p></li>
<li><p>json</p></li>
<li><p>markdown</p></li>
<li><p>btoa</p></li>
<li><p>base64encode</p></li>
<li><p>pairwise</p></li>
<li><p>numDiff</p></li>
<li><p>percentChange</p></li>
<li><p>groupByKeys</p></li>
<li><p>all</p></li>
<li><p>parseDate</p></li>
<li><p>parseDuration</p></li>
<li><p>parseUnixTimestamp</p></li>
</ol>
<p>Each of these extensions is detailed below with example usages.</p>
</section>
<section id="get">
<h2>1. get<a class="headerlink" href="#get" title="Link to this heading"></a></h2>
<p>Gets a value from an object by key. Equivalent to dot notation (obj.key) but can be used when the key is dynamic.</p>
<p>Examples:</p>
<div class="highlight-javascript notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><span class="nx">get</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="nx">data</span><span class="p">,</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s1">&#39;name&#39;</span><span class="p">)</span><span class="w">              </span><span class="c1">// Output: &quot;John Doe&quot;</span>
<span class="nx">get</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="nx">data</span><span class="p">,</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s1">&#39;age&#39;</span><span class="p">)</span><span class="w">               </span><span class="c1">// Output: 35</span>
<span class="nx">get</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="nx">data</span><span class="p">,</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s1">&#39;email&#39;</span><span class="p">)</span><span class="w">             </span><span class="c1">// Output: &quot;john.doe@example.com&quot;</span>
<span class="nx">get</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="nx">data</span><span class="p">,</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s1">&#39;tags[0]&#39;</span><span class="p">)</span><span class="w">           </span><span class="c1">// Output: &quot;frontend&quot;</span>
<span class="nx">get</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="nx">data</span><span class="p">,</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s1">&#39;projects[1].title&#39;</span><span class="p">)</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="c1">// Output: &quot;Project B&quot;</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
</section>
<section id="set">
<h2>2. set<a class="headerlink" href="#set" title="Link to this heading"></a></h2>
<p>Sets a value on an object by key. Note that the Kendraio mapping block doesn’t support modifying the original data, so you should use this with merge().</p>
<p>Examples:</p>
<div class="highlight-javascript notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><span class="nx">data</span><span class="p">.</span><span class="nx">merge</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="err">@</span><span class="p">,</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="p">{</span><span class="nx">jobTitle</span><span class="o">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="nx">set</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="err">@</span><span class="p">,</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s1">&#39;jobTitle&#39;</span><span class="p">,</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s1">&#39;Software Developer&#39;</span><span class="p">)})</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
</section>
<section id="findindex">
<h2>3. findIndex<a class="headerlink" href="#findindex" title="Link to this heading"></a></h2>
<p>Finds the index of the first element in an array that matches a given value.</p>
<p>Examples:</p>
<div class="highlight-javascript notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><span class="nx">findIndex</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="nx">data</span><span class="p">.</span><span class="nx">projects</span><span class="p">,</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="p">{</span><span class="nx">title</span><span class="o">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="sb">`Project A`</span><span class="p">})</span><span class="w">  </span><span class="c1">// Output: 0</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
</section>
<section id="uuid">
<h2>4. uuid<a class="headerlink" href="#uuid" title="Link to this heading"></a></h2>
<p>Generates a UUID (Universally Unique IDentifier).</p>
<p>Examples:</p>
<div class="highlight-javascript notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><span class="nx">uuid</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="sb">`test`</span><span class="p">)</span><span class="w">  </span><span class="c1">// Output: &quot;3ab8d0cd-7b76-5741-8bc9-5725650dc435&quot;</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
</section>
<section id="tolower">
<h2>5. toLower<a class="headerlink" href="#tolower" title="Link to this heading"></a></h2>
<p>Converts a string to lowercase.</p>
<p>Examples:</p>
<div class="highlight-javascript notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><span class="nx">toLower</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="nx">data</span><span class="p">.</span><span class="nx">name</span><span class="p">)</span><span class="w">  </span><span class="c1">// Output: &quot;john doe&quot;</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
</section>
<section id="toupper">
<h2>6. toUpper<a class="headerlink" href="#toupper" title="Link to this heading"></a></h2>
<p>Converts a string to uppercase.</p>
<p>Examples:</p>
<div class="highlight-javascript notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><span class="nx">toUpper</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="nx">data</span><span class="p">.</span><span class="nx">name</span><span class="p">)</span><span class="w">  </span><span class="c1">// Output: &quot;JOHN DOE&quot;</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
</section>
<section id="replace">
<h2>7. replace<a class="headerlink" href="#replace" title="Link to this heading"></a></h2>
<p>Returns a string replacing the first instance of a substring with another substring.</p>
<p>Examples:</p>
<div class="highlight-javascript notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><span class="nx">replace</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="nx">data</span><span class="p">.</span><span class="nx">name</span><span class="p">,</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s1">&#39;o&#39;</span><span class="p">,</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s1">&#39;-&#39;</span><span class="p">)</span><span class="w">        </span><span class="c1">// Output: &quot;J-hn Doe&quot;</span>
<span class="nx">replace</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="nx">data</span><span class="p">.</span><span class="nx">email</span><span class="p">,</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s1">&#39;.&#39;</span><span class="p">,</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s1">&#39;&#39;</span><span class="p">)</span><span class="w">        </span><span class="c1">// Output: &quot;johndoe@example.com&quot;</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
</section>
<section id="replaceall">
<h2>8. replaceAll<a class="headerlink" href="#replaceall" title="Link to this heading"></a></h2>
<p>Returns a string replacing all instances of a substring with another substring.</p>
<p>Examples:</p>
<div class="highlight-javascript notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><span class="nx">replaceAll</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="nx">data</span><span class="p">.</span><span class="nx">name</span><span class="p">,</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s1">&#39;o&#39;</span><span class="p">,</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s1">&#39;-&#39;</span><span class="p">)</span><span class="w">        </span><span class="c1">// Output: &quot;J-hn D-e&quot;</span>
<span class="nx">replaceAll</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="nx">data</span><span class="p">.</span><span class="nx">email</span><span class="p">,</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s1">&#39;.&#39;</span><span class="p">,</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s1">&#39;&#39;</span><span class="p">)</span><span class="w">        </span><span class="c1">// Output: &quot;johndoe@examplecom&quot;</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
</section>
<section id="trim">
<h2>9. trim<a class="headerlink" href="#trim" title="Link to this heading"></a></h2>
<p>Removes whitespace from both ends of a string.</p>
<p>Examples:</p>
<div class="highlight-javascript notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><span class="nx">trim</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s1">&#39;  John Doe  &#39;</span><span class="p">)</span><span class="w">  </span><span class="c1">// Output: &quot;John Doe&quot;</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
</section>
<section id="now">
<h2>10. now<a class="headerlink" href="#now" title="Link to this heading"></a></h2>
<p>Returns the current UTC timestamp in RFC 7231 format.</p>
<p>Examples:</p>
<div class="highlight-javascript notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><span class="nx">now</span><span class="p">()</span><span class="w">  </span><span class="c1">// Output: &quot;Wed, 07 Jun 2023 17:10:40&quot;</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
</section>
<section id="formatdate">
<h2>11. formatDate<a class="headerlink" href="#formatdate" title="Link to this heading"></a></h2>
<p>Formats a date string using a format string.</p>
<p>Examples:</p>
<div class="highlight-javascript notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><span class="nx">formatDate</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s1">&#39;2020-01-01&#39;</span><span class="p">,</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s1">&#39;dd/MM/yyyy&#39;</span><span class="p">)</span><span class="w">  </span><span class="c1">// Output: &quot;01/01/2020&quot;</span>

<span class="nx">formatDate</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s1">&#39;2020-01-01T14:50:00.000+01:00&#39;</span><span class="p">,</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s1">&#39;dd/MM/yyyy&#39;</span><span class="p">)</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="c1">// Output: &quot;01/01/2020&quot;</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
</section>
<section id="omit">
<h2>12. omit<a class="headerlink" href="#omit" title="Link to this heading"></a></h2>
<p>Creates an object composed of enumerable properties from the input objects omitting the properties named in the <a href="#id1"><span class="problematic" id="id2">*</span></a>names array.</p>
<p>Examples:</p>
<div class="highlight-javascript notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><span class="nx">omit</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="nx">data</span><span class="p">,</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="p">[</span><span class="s1">&#39;age&#39;</span><span class="p">,</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s1">&#39;email&#39;</span><span class="p">])</span>

<span class="c1">// Output:</span>
<span class="c1">// {</span>
<span class="c1">//   &quot;name&quot;: &quot;John Doe&quot;,</span>
<span class="c1">//   &quot;tags&quot;: [&quot;frontend&quot;, &quot;ui&quot;],</span>
<span class="c1">//   &quot;projects&quot;: [</span>
<span class="c1">//     {&quot;id&quot;: 1, &quot;title&quot;: &quot;Project A&quot;},</span>
<span class="c1">//     {&quot;id&quot;: 2, &quot;title&quot;: &quot;Project B&quot;}</span>
<span class="c1">//   ]</span>
<span class="c1">// }</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
</section>
<section id="pick">
<h2>13. pick<a class="headerlink" href="#pick" title="Link to this heading"></a></h2>
<p>Creates an object composed of the picked enumerable properties of the input object.</p>
<p>Examples:</p>
<div class="highlight-javascript notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><span class="nx">pick</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="nx">data</span><span class="p">,</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="p">[</span><span class="s1">&#39;name&#39;</span><span class="p">,</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s1">&#39;age&#39;</span><span class="p">])</span><span class="w">  </span><span class="c1">// Output: { &quot;name&quot;: &quot;John Doe&quot;, &quot;age&quot;: 35}</span>
<span class="nx">pick</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="nx">data</span><span class="p">,</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="p">[</span><span class="s1">&#39;projects&#39;</span><span class="p">,</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s1">&#39;name&#39;</span><span class="p">])</span>

<span class="c1">// Output:</span>
<span class="c1">// {</span>
<span class="c1">//   &quot;projects&quot;: [</span>
<span class="c1">//     {</span>
<span class="c1">//       &quot;id&quot;: 1,</span>
<span class="c1">//       &quot;title&quot;: &quot;Project A&quot;</span>
<span class="c1">//     },</span>
<span class="c1">//     {</span>
<span class="c1">//       &quot;id&quot;: 2,</span>
<span class="c1">//       &quot;title&quot;: &quot;Project B&quot;</span>
<span class="c1">//     }</span>
<span class="c1">//   ],</span>
<span class="c1">//   &quot;name&quot;: &quot;John Doe&quot;</span>
<span class="c1">// }</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
</section>
<section id="split">
<h2>14. split<a class="headerlink" href="#split" title="Link to this heading"></a></h2>
<p>Splits a string into an array of strings by separating it on a specified separator string.</p>
<p>Examples:</p>
<div class="highlight-javascript notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><span class="nx">split</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="nx">data</span><span class="p">.</span><span class="nx">tags</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="mf">0</span><span class="p">],</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s1">&#39;e&#39;</span><span class="p">)</span><span class="w">  </span><span class="c1">// Output: [&quot;front&quot;, &quot;nd&quot;]</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
</section>
<section id="find">
<h2>15. find<a class="headerlink" href="#find" title="Link to this heading"></a></h2>
<p>Returns the first element in an array that passes a truth test.</p>
<p>Examples:</p>
<div class="highlight-javascript notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><span class="nx">find</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="nx">data</span><span class="p">.</span><span class="nx">projects</span><span class="p">,</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="sb">`title`</span><span class="p">,</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="sb">`Project A`</span><span class="p">)</span><span class="w">  </span><span class="c1">// Output: {&quot;id&quot;: 1, &quot;title&quot;: &quot;Project A&quot;}</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
</section>
<section id="compact">
<h2>16. compact<a class="headerlink" href="#compact" title="Link to this heading"></a></h2>
<p>Creates an array with non-null values.</p>
<p>Examples:</p>
<div class="highlight-javascript notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><span class="nx">compact</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="sb">`{&quot;name&quot;: &quot;John Doe&quot;, &quot;age&quot;: null, &quot;email&quot;: &quot;john.doe@example.com&quot;}`</span><span class="p">)</span>

<span class="c1">// Output:</span>
<span class="c1">// {</span>
<span class="c1">//   &quot;name&quot;: &quot;John Doe&quot;,</span>
<span class="c1">//   &quot;email&quot;: &quot;john.doe@example.com&quot;</span>
<span class="c1">// }</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
</section>
<section id="qs">
<h2>17. qs<a class="headerlink" href="#qs" title="Link to this heading"></a></h2>
<p>Stringifies an object into a query string.</p>
<p>Examples:</p>
<div class="highlight-javascript notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><span class="nx">qs</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="nx">data</span><span class="p">)</span>

<span class="c1">// Output: &quot;name=John%20Doe&amp;age=35&amp;email=john.doe%40example.com&amp;tags%5B0%5D=frontend&amp;tags%5B1%5D=ui&amp;projects%5B0%5D%5Bid%5D=1&amp;projects%5B0%5D%5Btitle%5D=Project%20A&amp;projects%5B1%5D%5Bid%5D=2&amp;projects%5B1%5D%5Btitle%5D=Project%20B&quot;</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
</section>
<section id="parseqs">
<h2>18. parseQs<a class="headerlink" href="#parseqs" title="Link to this heading"></a></h2>
<p>Parses a query string into an object.</p>
<p>Examples:</p>
<div class="highlight-javascript notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><span class="nx">parseQs</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="sb">`name=John%20Doe`</span><span class="p">)</span>

<span class="c1">// Output:</span>
<span class="c1">// {</span>
<span class="c1">//     &quot;name&quot;: &quot;John Doe&quot;</span>
<span class="c1">// }</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
</section>
<section id="zip">
<h2>19. zip<a class="headerlink" href="#zip" title="Link to this heading"></a></h2>
<p>Creates an array of elements from two arrays.</p>
<p>Examples:</p>
<div class="highlight-javascript notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><span class="nx">zip</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="sb">`[&quot;a&quot;, &quot;b&quot;, &quot;c&quot;]`</span><span class="p">,</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="sb">`[1, 2, 3]`</span><span class="p">)</span><span class="w">  </span><span class="c1">// Output: [[&quot;a&quot;, 1], [&quot;b&quot;, 2], [&quot;c&quot;, 3]]</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
</section>
<section id="debug">
<h2>20. debug<a class="headerlink" href="#debug" title="Link to this heading"></a></h2>
<p>Logs a value to the JavaScript console for debugging and also returns the answer. Check the browser console for the logged value.</p>
<p>Examples:</p>
<div class="highlight-javascript notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><span class="nx">debug</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="nx">data</span><span class="p">)</span>

<span class="c1">// Output:</span>
<span class="c1">// {</span>
<span class="c1">//     &quot;name&quot;: &quot;John Doe&quot;,</span>
<span class="c1">//     &quot;age&quot;: 35,</span>
<span class="c1">//     &quot;email&quot;: &quot;john.doe@example.com&quot;,</span>
<span class="c1">//     &quot;tags&quot;: [</span>
<span class="c1">//         &quot;frontend&quot;,</span>
<span class="c1">//         &quot;ui&quot;</span>
<span class="c1">//     ],</span>
<span class="c1">//     &quot;projects&quot;: [</span>
<span class="c1">//         {</span>
<span class="c1">//             &quot;id&quot;: 1,</span>
<span class="c1">//             &quot;title&quot;: &quot;Project A&quot;</span>
<span class="c1">//         },</span>
<span class="c1">//         {</span>
<span class="c1">//             &quot;id&quot;: 2,</span>
<span class="c1">//             &quot;title&quot;: &quot;Project B&quot;</span>
<span class="c1">//         }</span>
<span class="c1">//     ]</span>
<span class="c1">// }</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
</section>
<section id="json">
<h2>21. json<a class="headerlink" href="#json" title="Link to this heading"></a></h2>
<p>Converts a value to a JSON string.</p>
<p>Examples:</p>
<div class="highlight-javascript notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><span class="nx">json</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="nx">data</span><span class="p">)</span>

<span class="c1">// Output: &quot;{\&quot;name\&quot;:\&quot;John Doe\&quot;,\&quot;age\&quot;:35,\&quot;email\&quot;:\&quot;john.doe@example.com\&quot;,\&quot;tags\&quot;:[\&quot;frontend\&quot;,\&quot;ui\&quot;],\&quot;projects\&quot;:[{\&quot;id\&quot;:1,\&quot;title\&quot;:\&quot;Project A\&quot;},{\&quot;id\&quot;:2,\&quot;title\&quot;:\&quot;Project B\&quot;}]}&quot;</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
</section>
<section id="markdown">
<h2>22. markdown<a class="headerlink" href="#markdown" title="Link to this heading"></a></h2>
<p>Converts a markdown string to HTML.</p>
<p>Examples:</p>
<div class="highlight-javascript notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><span class="nx">markdown</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s1">&#39;## Header&#39;</span><span class="p">)</span><span class="w">  </span><span class="c1">// Output: &quot;&lt;h2 id=\&quot;header\&quot;&gt;Header&lt;/h2&gt;&quot;</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
</section>
<section id="btoa">
<h2>23. btoa<a class="headerlink" href="#btoa" title="Link to this heading"></a></h2>
<p>Encodes a string in base-64.</p>
<p>Examples:</p>
<div class="highlight-javascript notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><span class="nx">btoa</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="nx">data</span><span class="p">.</span><span class="nx">name</span><span class="p">)</span><span class="w">  </span><span class="c1">// Output: &quot;Sm9obiBEb2U=&quot;</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
</section>
<section id="base64encode">
<h2>24. base64encode<a class="headerlink" href="#base64encode" title="Link to this heading"></a></h2>
<p>Safely encodes a string in base-64.</p>
<p>Examples:</p>
<div class="highlight-javascript notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><span class="nx">base64encode</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="nx">data</span><span class="p">.</span><span class="nx">name</span><span class="p">)</span><span class="w">  </span><span class="c1">// Output: &quot;Sm9obiBEb2U=&quot;</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
</section>
<section id="pairwise">
<h2>25. pairwise<a class="headerlink" href="#pairwise" title="Link to this heading"></a></h2>
<p>Groups the elements of an array into pairs. The function outputs an array containing objects, where each object consists of two properties: “current” and “next”. The “current” property refers to the current element of the input array, while the “next” property refers to the next element in the input array. If there is no next element, the “next” property will be set to null.</p>
<div class="highlight-javascript notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><span class="nx">pairwise</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="sb">`[1, 2, 3, 4, 5]`</span><span class="p">)</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
<p>Output:</p>
<div class="highlight-json notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><span class="p">[</span>
<span class="w">    </span><span class="p">{</span>
<span class="w">        </span><span class="nt">&quot;current&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="mi">1</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">        </span><span class="nt">&quot;next&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="mi">2</span>
<span class="w">    </span><span class="p">},</span>
<span class="w">    </span><span class="p">{</span>
<span class="w">        </span><span class="nt">&quot;current&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="mi">2</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">        </span><span class="nt">&quot;next&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="mi">3</span>
<span class="w">    </span><span class="p">},</span>
<span class="w">    </span><span class="p">{</span>
<span class="w">        </span><span class="nt">&quot;current&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="mi">3</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">        </span><span class="nt">&quot;next&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="mi">4</span>
<span class="w">    </span><span class="p">},</span>
<span class="w">    </span><span class="p">{</span>
<span class="w">        </span><span class="nt">&quot;current&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="mi">4</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">        </span><span class="nt">&quot;next&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="mi">5</span>
<span class="w">    </span><span class="p">},</span>
<span class="w">    </span><span class="p">{</span>
<span class="w">        </span><span class="nt">&quot;current&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="mi">5</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">        </span><span class="nt">&quot;next&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="kc">null</span>
<span class="w">    </span><span class="p">}</span>
<span class="p">]</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
</section>
<section id="numdiff">
<h2>26. numDiff<a class="headerlink" href="#numdiff" title="Link to this heading"></a></h2>
<p>Subtracts two numbers.</p>
<div class="highlight-javascript notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><span class="nx">numDiff</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="sb">`1`</span><span class="p">,</span><span class="sb">`0`</span><span class="p">)</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
<p>Output: 1</p>
</section>
<section id="percentchange">
<h2>27. percentChange<a class="headerlink" href="#percentchange" title="Link to this heading"></a></h2>
<p>Calculates the percent change between two numbers.</p>
<div class="highlight-javascript notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><span class="nx">percentChange</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="sb">`0.5`</span><span class="p">,</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="sb">`1`</span><span class="p">)</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
<p>Output: 100</p>
</section>
<section id="groupbykeys">
<h2>28. groupByKeys<a class="headerlink" href="#groupbykeys" title="Link to this heading"></a></h2>
<p>Groups the values of an array of objects by key.</p>
<div class="highlight-javascript notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><span class="nx">groupByKeys</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="nx">data</span><span class="p">.</span><span class="nx">projects</span><span class="p">)</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
<p>Output:</p>
<div class="highlight-json notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><span class="p">{</span>
<span class="w">    </span><span class="nt">&quot;id&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="p">[</span>
<span class="w">        </span><span class="mi">1</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">        </span><span class="mi">2</span>
<span class="w">    </span><span class="p">],</span>
<span class="w">    </span><span class="nt">&quot;title&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="p">[</span>
<span class="w">        </span><span class="s2">&quot;Project A&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">        </span><span class="s2">&quot;Project B&quot;</span>
<span class="w">    </span><span class="p">]</span>
<span class="p">}</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
</section>
<section id="all">
<h2>29. all<a class="headerlink" href="#all" title="Link to this heading"></a></h2>
<p>Checks if all elements in an array pass a test (i.e., are truthy).</p>
<div class="highlight-javascript notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><span class="nx">all</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="sb">`[true,1]`</span><span class="p">)</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
<p>Output: true</p>
<div class="highlight-javascript notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><span class="nx">all</span><span class="p">([</span><span class="sb">`true`</span><span class="p">,</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="sb">`false`</span><span class="p">,</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="sb">`1`</span><span class="p">,</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="sb">`example`</span><span class="p">])</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
<p>Output: false</p>
</section>
<section id="parsedate">
<h2>30. parseDate<a class="headerlink" href="#parsedate" title="Link to this heading"></a></h2>
<p>Parses a date string in various formats and returns a date string.</p>
<div class="highlight-javascript notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><span class="nx">parseDate</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s1">&#39;2020-01-01&#39;</span><span class="p">)</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
<p>Output: “2020-01-01T00:00:00.000+00:00”</p>
</section>
<section id="parseduration">
<h2>31. parseDuration<a class="headerlink" href="#parseduration" title="Link to this heading"></a></h2>
<p>Parses a duration string and returns the number of seconds.</p>
<div class="highlight-javascript notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><span class="nx">parseDuration</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s1">&#39;1:30:45&#39;</span><span class="p">)</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
<p>Output: “6030”</p>
</section>
<section id="parseunixtimestamp">
<h2>32. parseUnixTimestamp<a class="headerlink" href="#parseunixtimestamp" title="Link to this heading"></a></h2>
<p>Parses a Unix timestamp and returns an ISO 8601 date string.</p>
<div class="highlight-javascript notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><span class="nx">parseUnixTimestamp</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="sb">`0`</span><span class="p">)</span><span class="w">  </span><span class="c1">// Seconds</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
<p>Output: “1970-01-01T01:00:00.000+01:00”</p>
<div class="highlight-javascript notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><span class="nx">parseUnixTimestamp</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="sb">`1589756000000`</span><span class="p">,</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="sb">`ms`</span><span class="p">)</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="c1">// Milliseconds</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
<p>Output: “2020-05-19T03:00:00.000+01:00”</p>
</section>
</section>

  <section id="multiplex">
<h1>Multiplex<a class="headerlink" href="#multiplex" title="Link to this heading"></a></h1>
<p>Display and process multiple workflows concurrently.</p>
<section id="default-config">
<h2>Default config<a class="headerlink" href="#default-config" title="Link to this heading"></a></h2>
<div class="highlight-json notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><span class="p">{</span>
<span class="w">  </span><span class="nt">&quot;type&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;multi&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">  </span><span class="nt">&quot;batches&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="p">[]</span>
<span class="p">}</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
</section>
</section>

<li class="toctree-l1"><a class="reference internal" href="web_money.html">Web Money</a></li>
<li class="toctree-l1"><a class="reference internal" href="wallet.html">Wallet</a></li>
</ul>
<p class="caption" role="heading"><span class="caption-text">Automated testing</span></p>
<ul>
<li class="toctree-l1"><a class="reference internal" href="../../testing/tips.html">Notes for Cypress testing</a></li>
</ul>

        </div>
      </div>
    </nav>

    <section data-toggle="wy-nav-shift" class="wy-nav-content-wrap"><nav class="wy-nav-top" aria-label="Mobile navigation menu" >
          <i data-toggle="wy-nav-top" class="fa fa-bars"></i>
          <a href="../../index.html">Kendraio App</a>
      </nav>

      <div class="wy-nav-content">
        <div class="rst-content style-external-links">
          <div role="navigation" aria-label="Page navigation">
  <ul class="wy-breadcrumbs">
      <li><a href="../../index.html" class="icon icon-home" aria-label="Home"></a></li>
      <li class="breadcrumb-item active">Context &amp; State</li>
      <li class="wy-breadcrumbs-aside">
            <a href="../../_sources/workflow/blocks/context_block.rst.txt" rel="nofollow"> View page source</a>
      </li>
  </ul>
  <hr/>
</div>
          <div role="main" class="document" itemscope="itemscope" itemtype="http://schema.org/Article">
           <div itemprop="articleBody">
             
  <section id="context-state">
<h1>Context &amp; State<a class="headerlink" href="#context-state" title="Link to this heading"></a></h1>
<dl class="simple">
<dt>Context and state are in-memory, temporary datastores. Use the context block to save data into the flow context or global state to make it available for all</dt><dd><p>tasks.</p>
</dd>
</dl>
<p>There are two top level storage domains that can be accessed with this block.</p>
<p><strong>Context</strong> is passed from block to block. When processing data in parrallel - during a batch or multiplex flow - context will be different in each branch.
Blocks will only have access to values written by another block in the same branch.</p>
<p><strong>State</strong> is shared by all blocks. The content of state is stored in memory and is available to any subsequent flows - but only on the same tab until that tab is closed or reloaded. There are two possible ways to address the content of state:
<strong>state.global</strong> contains all data stored in the state.
<strong>state.local</strong> is a dynamic sub-branch of the global state based on the current url. (eg. on path “x/y” state.local is the equivalent of “state.global.x.y”)
<strong>state.flags</strong> provides an additional, top level shortcut to state information. Flags is intended to be used to store small units of information
that do not belong inside the standard url hierarchy.</p>
<p>For more detailed information, see the State section below.</p>
<section id="default-config">
<h2>Default config<a class="headerlink" href="#default-config" title="Link to this heading"></a></h2>
<div class="highlight-json notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><span class="p">{</span>
<span class="w">  </span><span class="nt">&quot;type&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;context-save&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">  </span><span class="nt">&quot;key&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;saved&quot;</span>
<span class="p">}</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
</section>
<section id="supported-properties">
<h2>Supported properties<a class="headerlink" href="#supported-properties" title="Link to this heading"></a></h2>
<ul>
<li><p><strong>key</strong> (string) The key of the value that will be saved into context.
For example, if you use a key of “saved” then the value will be available in the context as “context.saved”.</p>
<p>When context key starts with “state”, values will be written to the shared state storage. State paths should
begin with “state.local” or “state.global”. If the a key other than “local” or “global” follows “state”, local will be assumed.
ie. “state.key” is the equivalent of “state.local.key”. (See State below)</p>
<p>A previous version of the key was <em>contextKey</em>, which is still accepted as a configuration variable.
If both <em>key</em> and <em>contextKey</em> exist in config, <em>key</em> will override.</p>
</li>
<li><p><strong>valueGetter</strong> (string) [OPTIONAL]  The JMESPath of the value to store into context.
This defaults to “data” so if it is omitted it stores the whole incoming value into context.
JMESPath allows for direct manipulation of data in the expression.
This allows a <strong>valueGetter</strong> to read, transform and write data in a single statement.</p></li>
<li><p><strong>keyGetter</strong> (string) [OPTIONAL] - the JMESPath of the key to use within context.
JMESPath does not allow for variable keys, so this makes it possible for batch code to
write to different context locations.</p></li>
</ul>
</section>
<section id="usage-patterns">
<h2>Usage patterns<a class="headerlink" href="#usage-patterns" title="Link to this heading"></a></h2>
<p>The most common usage pattern with this block is to use it with the
variable get block, to fetch a value from the adapter storage and save it
into the context.</p>
</section>
<section id="working-with-state">
<h2>Working with State<a class="headerlink" href="#working-with-state" title="Link to this heading"></a></h2>
<p>State is a shared storage. Values written to state will last for a single user session, and are available to any flow.</p>
<section id="persistence">
<h3>Persistence<a class="headerlink" href="#persistence" title="Link to this heading"></a></h3>
<p>State data is not persistent. It only exists in memory for a specific tab and is lost on a reload.</p>
</section>
<section id="local-and-global-state">
<h3>Local and global State<a class="headerlink" href="#local-and-global-state" title="Link to this heading"></a></h3>
<p>It is assumed that the state storage will mirror the site structure. Flows should generally write to and read from <em>state.local</em>.
Internally <em>state.local</em> will be translated to a path to mirror the current url.</p>
<p>When on a the path <em>/workflowCloud/listWorkflows</em>, writing to <em>state.local.filter</em> will be translated to <em>workflowCloud.listWorkflows.filter</em>.
This internal, translated path is the global state path, and it can also be accessed directly via <em>state.global</em>.</p>
<p>When a path is prefixed with <em>state.global</em>, it will allow values written to any part of the tree. Writing to <em>state.global.filter</em> will be translated to
<em>filter</em>. Writing to <em>state.global.workflowCloud.listWorkflows.filter</em> will update <em>workflowCloud.listWorkflows.filter</em>.</p>
<p>When a value is written to a location other than “local” or “global”, “local” will be assumed.
When on the same url as above, writing to “state.filter” will get translated to <em>workflowCloud.listWorkflows.filter</em>.</p>
</section>
<section id="flags">
<h3>Flags<a class="headerlink" href="#flags" title="Link to this heading"></a></h3>
<p>Flags exist within the global state, but can be accessed directly via this “shortcut”. The purpose of the flag address is to create a standard
storage location for fragments of data that can be easily accessed and modified by different components.</p>
<p>Flags are available at the top level of the state to make it easy to quickly check their values to enable and disable functionality.</p>
<div class="highlight-Json notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><span class="p">{</span>
<span class="w">   </span><span class="nt">&quot;type&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="s2">&quot;debug&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">   </span><span class="nt">&quot;enabledGetter&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="s2">&quot;flags.devel&quot;</span>
<span class="p">}</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
<section id="suggested-standard-flags">
<h4>Suggested Standard Flags<a class="headerlink" href="#suggested-standard-flags" title="Link to this heading"></a></h4>
<ul class="simple">
<li><p>devel - Set this flag to true to signify that development elements of a flow should be enabled.</p></li>
</ul>
</section>
</section>
<section id="security-and-access-control">
<h3>Security and access control<a class="headerlink" href="#security-and-access-control" title="Link to this heading"></a></h3>
<p>There is currently no access control or protection in state. Any path in the global state is accessible (read and write) to any flow.
In the future some form of restriction may be implemented.</p>
</section>
</section>
<section id="see-also">
<h2>See also<a class="headerlink" href="#see-also" title="Link to this heading"></a></h2>
<ul class="simple">
<li><p><span class="xref std std-ref">context_runner_block</span> - The block that runs flows in their own context and then runs another seperate flow.</p></li>
</ul>
</section>
</section>

  <ul class="wy-breadcrumbs">
      <li><a href="../../index.html" class="icon icon-home" aria-label="Home"></a></li>
      <li class="breadcrumb-item active">Chart</li>
      <li class="wy-breadcrumbs-aside">
            <a href="../../_sources/workflow/blocks/chart.rst.txt" rel="nofollow"> View page source</a>
      </li>

  <hr/>
</div>
          <div role="main" class="document" itemscope="itemscope" itemtype="http://schema.org/Article">
           <div itemprop="articleBody">
             
  <section id="chart">
<h1>Chart<a class="headerlink" href="#chart" title="Link to this heading"></a></h1>
<p>Display values in a configurable chart.</p>
<section id="default-config">
<h2>Default config<a class="headerlink" href="#default-config" title="Link to this heading"></a></h2>
<div class="highlight-json notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><span class="p">{</span>
<span class="w">  </span><span class="nt">&quot;type&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;chart&quot;</span>
<span class="p">}</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
</section>
<section id="supported-properties">
<h2>Supported properties<a class="headerlink" href="#supported-properties" title="Link to this heading"></a></h2>
<ul class="simple">
<li><p><strong>chartType</strong> (default = “doughnut”): Set this to any of the chart.js chart types to switch the type of chart output.</p></li>
<li><p><strong>options</strong> (default = {}): Options to pass along to chart.js - the allowed options are different depending on the chart type. More details can be found in the Chart.js documentation: <a class="reference external" href="https://www.chartjs.org/docs/latest/charts/">https://www.chartjs.org/docs/latest/charts/</a></p></li>
</ul>
<p>In order to successfully render charts, the input data to this block must match the format of the data expected by the chart type. The mapping block is useful to get data into the correct format for display. Support for all chart types is in progress.</p>
<p>Pie and Doughnut chart types require a list of objects, where each object has a “label” and a “value”.</p>
<p>A chart block is usually preceded by a mapping block to get the data into the correct format. Useful examples are given here for each chart type that is supported:</p>
</section>
<section id="pie-and-doughnut-charts">
<h2>Pie and Doughnut charts<a class="headerlink" href="#pie-and-doughnut-charts" title="Link to this heading"></a></h2>
<div class="highlight-json notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><span class="p">{</span>
<span class="w">  </span><span class="nt">&quot;type&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;mapping&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">  </span><span class="nt">&quot;mapping&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;data[].{ label: originalTitle, value: length(associatedISRCs) }&quot;</span>
<span class="p">}</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
<p>(NB: more mapping examples will be added here as support for more chart types is added)</p>
</section>
<section id="examples">
<h2>Examples<a class="headerlink" href="#examples" title="Link to this heading"></a></h2>
<p>The Chart.JS options can be customised.
Callback functions in the configuration are not supported.
Instead, you can use a JMES Path expression
anywhere in the Chart.JS options that expects a callback function.
For example, to use a logarithmic
scale, customise the <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">yAxes</span></code> property:</p>
<div class="highlight-json notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><span class="p">{</span>
<span class="w">    </span><span class="nt">&quot;type&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;chart&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">    </span><span class="nt">&quot;chartType&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;line&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">    </span><span class="nt">&quot;multi&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="kc">true</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">    </span><span class="nt">&quot;options&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="p">{</span>
<span class="w">        </span><span class="nt">&quot;scales&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="p">{</span>
<span class="w">            </span><span class="nt">&quot;yAxes&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="p">[</span>
<span class="w">                </span><span class="p">{</span>
<span class="w">                    </span><span class="nt">&quot;type&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;logarithmic&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">                    </span><span class="nt">&quot;ticks&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="p">{</span>
<span class="w">                        </span><span class="nt">&quot;callback&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;value&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">                        </span><span class="nt">&quot;maxTicksLimit&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="mi">10</span>
<span class="w">                    </span><span class="p">}</span>
<span class="w">                </span><span class="p">}</span>
<span class="w">            </span><span class="p">]</span>
<span class="w">        </span><span class="p">}</span>
<span class="w">    </span><span class="p">}</span>
<span class="p">}</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
<p>The chart block can be configured to plot multiple sets of data on the same chart.
To enabled this, set <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">multi</span></code> to true:</p>
<div class="highlight-json notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><span class="p">{</span>
<span class="w">    </span><span class="nt">&quot;type&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;chart&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">    </span><span class="nt">&quot;chartType&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;line&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">    </span><span class="nt">&quot;multi&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="kc">true</span>
<span class="p">}</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
<p>Then in the mapping that prepares the data for the chart block, create an
array of data sets. Either using a <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">batch</span></code> block to query multiple sources
or a single mapping that produces an array of data sets from a single source.
Each dataset contains a <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">data</span></code> key and <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">label</span></code>.
Here is an example of a query against multiple sources:</p>
<div class="highlight-json notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><span class="p">{</span>
<span class="w">    </span><span class="nt">&quot;type&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;multi&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">    </span><span class="nt">&quot;batches&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="p">[</span>
<span class="w">        </span><span class="p">{</span>
<span class="w">            </span><span class="nt">&quot;blocks&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="p">[</span>
<span class="w">                </span><span class="p">{</span>
<span class="w">                    </span><span class="nt">&quot;type&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;http&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">                    </span><span class="nt">&quot;method&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;get&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">                    </span><span class="nt">&quot;endpoint&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;https://trends-api.now.sh/api?keyword=angular&quot;</span>
<span class="w">                </span><span class="p">},</span>
<span class="w">                </span><span class="p">{</span>
<span class="w">                    </span><span class="nt">&quot;type&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;mapping&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">                    </span><span class="nt">&quot;mapping&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;{ data: data.default.timelineData[].{ value: value[0], label: formattedAxisTime }, label: &#39;angular&#39;}&quot;</span>
<span class="w">                </span><span class="p">}</span>
<span class="w">            </span><span class="p">]</span>
<span class="w">        </span><span class="p">},</span>
<span class="w">        </span><span class="p">{</span>
<span class="w">            </span><span class="nt">&quot;blocks&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="p">[</span>
<span class="w">                </span><span class="p">{</span>
<span class="w">                    </span><span class="nt">&quot;type&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;http&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">                    </span><span class="nt">&quot;method&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;get&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">                    </span><span class="nt">&quot;endpoint&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;https://trends-api.now.sh/api?keyword=react&quot;</span>
<span class="w">                </span><span class="p">},</span>
<span class="w">                </span><span class="p">{</span>
<span class="w">                    </span><span class="nt">&quot;type&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;mapping&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">                    </span><span class="nt">&quot;mapping&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;{ data: data.default.timelineData[].{ value: value[0], label: formattedAxisTime }, label: &#39;react&#39;}&quot;</span>
<span class="w">                </span><span class="p">}</span>
<span class="w">            </span><span class="p">]</span>
<span class="w">        </span><span class="p">},</span>
<span class="w">        </span><span class="p">{</span>
<span class="w">            </span><span class="nt">&quot;blocks&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="p">[</span>
<span class="w">                </span><span class="p">{</span>
<span class="w">                    </span><span class="nt">&quot;type&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;http&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">                    </span><span class="nt">&quot;method&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;get&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">                    </span><span class="nt">&quot;endpoint&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;https://trends-api.now.sh/api?keyword=vue&quot;</span>
<span class="w">                </span><span class="p">},</span>
<span class="w">                </span><span class="p">{</span>
<span class="w">                    </span><span class="nt">&quot;type&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;mapping&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">                    </span><span class="nt">&quot;mapping&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;{ data: data.default.timelineData[].{ value: value[0], label: formattedAxisTime }, label: &#39;vue&#39;}&quot;</span>
<span class="w">                </span><span class="p">}</span>
<span class="w">            </span><span class="p">]</span>
<span class="w">        </span><span class="p">}</span>
<span class="w">    </span><span class="p">]</span>
<span class="p">}</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
</section>
</section>

  <hr/>
</div>
          <div role="main" class="document" itemscope="itemscope" itemtype="http://schema.org/Article">
           <div itemprop="articleBody">
             
  <section id="file-export">
<h1>File Export<a class="headerlink" href="#file-export" title="Link to this heading"></a></h1>
<p>Export data to a file.</p>
<section id="expected-input-data">
<h2>Expected input data<a class="headerlink" href="#expected-input-data" title="Link to this heading"></a></h2>
<p>This block expects the input data to contain the following keys. These match the output of the serialize block, so the two blocks can be easily combined to output data:</p>
<ul class="simple">
<li><p><strong>format</strong>: the format of the data</p></li>
<li><p><strong>data</strong>: the data serialized as a text string</p></li>
</ul>
<p>If the input data is a string then it will be exported as a plain text file. If the input data is an object, but does not contain the “type” and “data” keys then it will be exported as JSON data.</p>
</section>
<section id="default-config">
<h2>Default config<a class="headerlink" href="#default-config" title="Link to this heading"></a></h2>
<div class="highlight-json notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><span class="p">{</span>
<span class="w">  </span><span class="nt">&quot;type&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;file-export&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">  </span><span class="nt">&quot;label&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;Export&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">  </span><span class="nt">&quot;fileName&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;exported&quot;</span>
<span class="p">}</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
</section>
<section id="supported-properties">
<h2>Supported properties<a class="headerlink" href="#supported-properties" title="Link to this heading"></a></h2>
<ul class="simple">
<li><p><strong>label</strong> (default = “Export”) the label to use for the export button</p></li>
<li><p><strong>fileName</strong> (default = “export”) the name of the downloaded file, the file extension will be added automatically based on the input data format.</p></li>
</ul>
</section>
</section>


           </div>
          </div>

<p class="caption" role="heading"><span class="caption-text">Adapters</span></p>
<ul>
<li class="toctree-l1"><a class="reference internal" href="../../adapters/intro.html">Adapters</a></li>

<li class="toctree-l1"><a class="reference internal" href="../../adapters/creating.html">Creating an Adapter</a></li>
</ul>
<p class="caption" role="heading"><span class="caption-text">Form Builder</span></p>
<ul>

          <div role="main" class="document" itemscope="itemscope" itemtype="http://schema.org/Article">
           <div itemprop="articleBody">
             
  <section id="wallet">
<h1>Wallet<a class="headerlink" href="#wallet" title="Link to this heading"></a></h1>
<p>The Wallet block allows Uphold users to:
- list all their Uphold XRP cards
- checking balances of an Uphold XRP ‘Card’, and
- make new XRP cards for use with ILP as used by Coil.</p>
<section id="example-of-retrieving-an-uphold-xrp-card-balance-for-a-given-ilp-address">
<h2>Example of retrieving an Uphold XRP card balance for a given ILP address:<a class="headerlink" href="#example-of-retrieving-an-uphold-xrp-card-balance-for-a-given-ilp-address" title="Link to this heading"></a></h2>
<div class="highlight-json notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><span class="p">{</span>
<span class="w">  </span><span class="nt">&quot;type&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;wallet&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">    </span><span class="nt">&quot;service&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;uphold&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">    </span><span class="nt">&quot;get_balance&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="p">{</span>
<span class="w">        </span><span class="nt">&quot;ILP&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;$ilp-sandbox.uphold.com/Y9UDQn8UFKw6&quot;</span>
<span class="w">    </span><span class="p">}</span>
<span class="p">}</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
<p>The card balance is returned as the amount of XRP (from the native format of the asset), with data output to the flow as, which you can see using the debug block.</p>
</section>
<section id="example-of-a-user-retrieving-all-their-uphold-xrp-card-balances">
<h2>Example of a user retrieving all their Uphold XRP card balances:<a class="headerlink" href="#example-of-a-user-retrieving-all-their-uphold-xrp-card-balances" title="Link to this heading"></a></h2>
<div class="highlight-json notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><span class="p">{</span>
<span class="w">    </span><span class="nt">&quot;type&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;wallet&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">    </span><span class="nt">&quot;service&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;uphold&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">    </span><span class="nt">&quot;list_all_balances&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="kc">true</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">    </span><span class="nt">&quot;make_card_button&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="kc">true</span>
<span class="p">}</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
<p>The card balances are returned as a data object, with card ID’s as the keys, like this:</p>
<div class="highlight-json notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><span class="p">{</span>
<span class="w">    </span><span class="nt">&quot;aa4eb91212a34dde89bfaf7ecaba6bd0&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="p">{</span>
<span class="w">        </span><span class="nt">&quot;balance&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="mf">0.025001</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">        </span><span class="nt">&quot;ILP_URL&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;$ilp-sandbox.uphold.com/Y9UDQn8UFKw6&quot;</span>
<span class="w">    </span><span class="p">},</span>
<span class="w">    </span><span class="nt">&quot;efac641428c44e6ca981c73264beafc6&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="p">{</span>
<span class="w">        </span><span class="nt">&quot;balance&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="mi">0</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">        </span><span class="nt">&quot;ILP_URL&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;$ilp-sandbox.uphold.com/7rmLpyGAYBrq&quot;</span>
<span class="w">    </span><span class="p">}</span>
<span class="p">}</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
</section>
<section id="to-show-the-xrp-card-creation-button">
<h2>To show the XRP card creation button:<a class="headerlink" href="#to-show-the-xrp-card-creation-button" title="Link to this heading"></a></h2>
<div class="highlight-json notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><span class="p">{</span>
<span class="w">    </span><span class="nt">&quot;type&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;wallet&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">    </span><span class="nt">&quot;service&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;uphold&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">    </span><span class="nt">&quot;make_card_button&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="kc">true</span>
<span class="p">}</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
</section>
<section id="see-also">
<h2>See also:<a class="headerlink" href="#see-also" title="Link to this heading"></a></h2>
<p><a class="reference internal" href="web_money.html#web-money"><span class="std std-ref">Web Money</span></a> used by Coil users to stream XRP payments over the Interledger Protocol to an ILP address of a wallet.</p>
</section>
</section>

          <div role="main" class="document" itemscope="itemscope" itemtype="http://schema.org/Article">
           <div itemprop="articleBody">
             
  <section id="csv-import">
<h1>CSV Import<a class="headerlink" href="#csv-import" title="Link to this heading"></a></h1>
<p><strong>Deprecated, use separate file input and parse data blocks</strong></p>
<p>Load in data from a CSV or Excel file.</p>
<section id="default-config">
<h2>Default config<a class="headerlink" href="#default-config" title="Link to this heading"></a></h2>
<div class="highlight-json notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><span class="p">{</span>
<span class="w">  </span><span class="nt">&quot;type&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;csv-import&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">  </span><span class="nt">&quot;header&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="kc">true</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">  </span><span class="nt">&quot;skipEmptyLines&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="kc">true</span>
<span class="p">}</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
</section>
<section id="supported-properties">
<h2>Supported properties<a class="headerlink" href="#supported-properties" title="Link to this heading"></a></h2>
<ul class="simple">
<li><p>header (default = false): Set this to true and then the first row of parsed data will be interpreted as field names</p></li>
<li><p>skipEmptyLines (default = false): If true, lines that are completely empty (those which evaluate to an empty string) will be skipped. If set to ‘greedy’, lines that don’t have any content (those which have only whitespace after parsing) will also be skipped.</p></li>
</ul>
<p>Most of the other config properties can be omitted, as the default values will automatically detect the correct settings to use from the imported CSV file. Other supported properties:</p>
<ul class="simple">
<li><p>delimiter (default = “”): Leave blank to auto-detect from a list of most common delimiters, or any values passed in through delimitersToGuess</p></li>
<li><p>newline (default = “”): Leave blank to auto-detect. Must be one of r, n, or rn.</p></li>
<li><p>quoteChar (default = ‘”’)  The character used to quote fields. The quoting of all fields is not mandatory. Any field which is not quoted will correctly read.</p></li>
<li><p>escapeChar: ‘”’,</p></li>
<li><p>dynamicTyping (default = false): If true, numeric and boolean data will be converted to their type instead of remaining strings.</p></li>
<li><p>preview: 0 - if greater than 0 then only this many lines will be read</p></li>
<li><p>delimitersToGuess: [‘,’, ‘t’, ‘|’, ‘;’]</p></li>
</ul>
</section>
</section>


           </div>
          </div>
          <footer><div class="rst-footer-buttons" role="navigation" aria-label="Footer">
        <a href="csv_export.html" class="btn btn-neutral float-left" title="CSV Export" accesskey="p" rel="prev"><span class="fa fa-arrow-circle-left" aria-hidden="true"></span> Previous</a>
        <a href="db.html" class="btn btn-neutral float-right" title="Local Database" accesskey="n" rel="next">Next <span class="fa fa-arrow-circle-right" aria-hidden="true"></span></a>
    </div>

          <div role="main" class="document" itemscope="itemscope" itemtype="http://schema.org/Article">
           <div itemprop="articleBody">
             
  <section id="notes-for-cypress-testing">
<h1>Notes for Cypress testing<a class="headerlink" href="#notes-for-cypress-testing" title="Link to this heading"></a></h1>
<section id="https-not-http">
<h2>“https” not “http”<a class="headerlink" href="#https-not-http" title="Link to this heading"></a></h2>
<p>When tests are run within GitHub’s environments, calls to external urls must be https, otherwise they will not run.
This applies even to fixtures that don’t even make external requests.</p>
</section>
</section>
